gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The checker's **explicit expression-nesting budget** (the \"banked guard 4\" discipline; A4-02)."] # [doc = " Type-checking recurses on the expression AST; rather than rely on the host call stack to bound"] # [doc = " that recursion (a resource that varies by thread and by IR frame size — never a semantic limit),"] # [doc = " the checker carries this reified budget and refuses past it with a clean [`CheckError`], exactly as"] # [doc = " the parser ([`crate::parse::MAX_EXPR_DEPTH`]) and the evaluator (`eval::DEFAULT_DEPTH`) do for their"] # [doc = " recursions. It is set comfortably **above** the parser's surface-nesting cap (so it never trips for"] # [doc = " parser-produced ASTs — it is the defense-in-depth ceiling for an AST handed straight to the checker"] # [doc = " via the API), and the recursion runs on a deep worker stack ([`mycelium_stack`]) so this budget — not a"] # [doc = " host-stack overflow — is always what bounds a pathological input."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Grounding (measured, not guessed).** The 256 MiB worker stack physically supports **~24,600**"] # [doc = " levels of `check` recursion in a debug build (empirically: 24,589 survives, 24,765 aborts;"] # [doc = " ~10.9 KiB/frame — release frames are smaller, so the ceiling is *higher* there). This budget"] # [doc = " (`4096`) is therefore a **~6× safety margin** below the measured physical ceiling, and **16×**"] # [doc = " above the parser's 256-deep surface cap — so a real (parsed) program is never within ~16× of it,"] # [doc = " and even a synthetic AST refuses cleanly with ~6× stack headroom to spare. Raising it is safe up to"] # [doc = " roughly a third of the physical ceiling; widen the worker stack first if more is ever wanted."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Self-hosting:** this explicit budget is the portable primitive (it carries over to the"] # [doc = " Mycelium-native frontend's clocked bounded-computation model); the worker stack is the transitional"] # [doc = " Rust-only adapter (`mycelium_stack`)."] pub const MAX_CHECK_DEPTH : u32 = 4096 ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const MAX_CHECK_DEPTH` — no const item production in the grammar (`item` covers use/default/type/trait/impl/fn/object/lower/derive only)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " RAII depth accounting for the checker's recursive [`Cx::check`] (paired with [`MAX_CHECK_DEPTH`]):"] # [doc = " increments the live nesting depth on entry and decrements it on drop, so the budget is honoured on"] # [doc = " **every** exit path (early `return`, `?`, or fall-through) — never a counter that leaks on error."] struct DepthGuard < 'a > (& 'a Cell < u32 >) ;

Constructed by the `[e1, …]` list literal; the surface checks"] # [doc = " elements are homogeneous and the count matches `N` (never-silent — G2). Guarantee: `Exact`"] # [doc = " (a literal *is* its representation)."] Seq (Box < Ty > , u32) , # [doc = " `Bytes` — a first-class byte string (RFC-0032 D4; M-750). Constructed by the `0x…` literal."] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`."] Bytes , # [doc = " `Float` — the first-class scalar float, IEEE-754 binary64 (ADR-040; M-897). A nullary repr"] # [doc = " type like [`Ty::Bytes`] (the width set is F64-only at introduction — ADR-040 FLAG-1)."] # [doc = " Constructed by the decimal float literal (`1.5`), which denotes the **correctly-rounded**"] # [doc = " (RNE) binary64 of its decimal text (FLAG-3). Guarantee: `Exact` as a *definition* (the"] # [doc = " literal denotes exactly that binary64 value); the host-conversion claim is pinned"] # [doc = " `Empirical` by the round-trip conformance corpus (ADR-040 §2.6 — VR-5, no silent upgrade)."] Float , # [doc = " An **abstract type parameter** (a skolem variable) — in scope only while checking a generic"] # [doc = " declaration's constructors or a generic function's body (RFC-0007 §11.2). Two `Var`s are equal"] # [doc = " iff their names match; that structural equality is the engine of parametric checking. A"] # [doc = " `Var`-typed value is **representation-opaque**: no representation-specific `Op` may apply to it"] # [doc = " (this is the unbounded-case form of RFC-0019 §4.6's Repr-polymorphism restriction — it falls"] # [doc = " out of the abstract-variable discipline, restating S1 at the polymorphic level)."] Var (String) , # [doc = " A **function type** `A -> B` (RFC-0024 §3, M-686): the type of a named top-level function"] # [doc = " used as a first-class value. Stage-1 supports single-argument arrows only; the parameter"] # [doc = " type and return type may themselves be abstract (`Ty::Var`). A `Fn`-typed value is not a"] # [doc = " legal instance head (coherence is over concrete types — same posture as `Ty::Var`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Declared` (a type-level contract; no theorem — VR-5)."] Fn (Box < Ty > , Box < Ty >) , }

Baking here closes it for the DN-112 §10 item 2 exploit"] # [doc = " shape (`use a.use_t; type T = Mk(..); use_t(forge())`): `use_t`'s parameter type is permanently"] # [doc = " `a::T`, never re-derived from whichever nodule happens to be checking the call."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Scoped, honest best-effort (VR-5) — never a spurious refusal.** Resolved against the"] # [doc = " declaring nodule's **own** registered types only (no cross-nodule imports it itself might use —"] # [doc = " resolving those would need the whole phylum's imports settled first, a larger change). A"] # [doc = " signature that itself references a type the declaring nodule imports from elsewhere fails to"] # [doc = " resolve here; the caller (`Exports`-building loop) treats that as \"not bakeable\" and leaves the"] # [doc = " entry absent — `check_app` then falls back to the pre-existing (unbaked) re-resolution path for"] # [doc = " that one function, unchanged from before this fix (never a hard error from this best-effort"] # [doc = " step). This is a disclosed, narrower residual, not a silent gap."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " A [`CheckError`] iff a value-parameter or return `TypeRef` does not resolve against `types` —"] # [doc = " the caller treats this as \"not bakeable\" rather than propagating it."] pub (crate) fn resolve_fn_sig (types : & BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > , sig : & FnSig ,) -> Result < (Vec < Ty > , Ty) , CheckError > { let tyvars = sig . param_names () ; let mut params = Vec :: with_capacity (sig . value_params . len ()) ; for p in & sig . value_params { let (ty , _) = resolve_ty (& sig . name , types , & tyvars , & p . ty) ? ; params . push (ty) ; } let (ret , _) = resolve_ty (& sig . name , types , & tyvars , & sig . ret) ? ; Ok ((params , ret)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Insert the export `qual` (a `pub` type/fn/trait) into the per-nodule imports under `simple`."] # [doc = " Exactly one of the three export tables holds `qual` (a name is one kind); insert from whichever."] fn insert_export (imp : & mut NoduleImports , exports : & Exports , qual : & str , simple : & str) { let is_cross_phylum = qual . contains ("::") ; if let Some (info) = exports . types . get (qual) { imp . types . insert (simple . to_owned () , info . clone ()) ; if let Some (sealed) = exports . sealed_ctors . get (qual) { imp . sealed . extend (sealed . iter () . cloned ()) ; } } if let Some (fd) = exports . fns . get (qual) { imp . fns . insert (simple . to_owned () , fd . clone ()) ; if let Some (resolved) = exports . resolved_fn_sigs . get (qual) { imp . resolved_fn_sigs . insert (simple . to_owned () , resolved . clone ()) ; } if is_cross_phylum { imp . cross_phylum_fns . insert (simple . to_owned ()) ; } else { imp . cross_phylum_fns . remove (simple) ; } } if let Some (info) = exports . traits . get (qual) { imp . traits . insert (simple . to_owned () , info . clone ()) ; if is_cross_phylum { imp . cross_phylum_traits . insert (simple . to_owned ()) ; } else { imp . cross_phylum_traits . remove (simple) ; } } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Remove any import binding for `simple` across all three tables (used when a glob-vs-glob collision"] # [doc = " demotes a tentatively-bound name to `ambiguous`)."] fn remove_import (imp : & mut NoduleImports , simple : & str) { imp . types . remove (simple) ; imp . fns . remove (simple) ; imp . traits . remove (simple) ; imp . resolved_fn_sigs . remove (simple) ; imp . cross_phylum_traits . remove (simple) ; imp . cross_phylum_fns . remove (simple) ; }

Definitions"] # [doc = " marked `thaw` are exempt from the gate (RFC-0017 §4.3). When `matured_scope` is `false` this"] # [doc = " is identical to [`check_nodule`]."] pub fn check_nodule_matured (nodule : & Nodule , matured_scope : bool) -> Result < Env , CheckError > { check_and_resolve_matured (nodule , matured_scope) . map (| (env , _) | env) }

gaps.snippet: fn check_and_resolve_matured_inner (nodule : & Nodule , matured_scope : bool ,) -> Result < (Env , Nodule) , CheckError > { let resolved = crate :: ambient :: resolve (nodule) ? ; let phylum = Phylum :: of_one (resolved . clone ()) ; let (penv , _exports) = check_phylum_inner (& phylum , & Phyla :: default () , matured_scope) ? ; let env = penv . single () . expect ("a phylum-of-one yields exactly one Env") . clone () ; let mut items = Vec :: with_capacity (resolved . items . len ()) ; for item in & resolved . items { match item { Item :: Fn (fd) => { let resolved_fd = env . fns . get (& fd . sig . name) . cloned () . unwrap_or_else (| | fd . clone ()) ; items . push (Item :: Fn (resolved_fd)) ; } other => items . push (other . clone ()) , } } let twin = Nodule { path : resolved . path . clone () , std_sys : resolved . std_sys , items , } ; Ok ((env , twin)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Register a (resolved) nodule's **data declarations** into `types` (Pass 1; RFC-0007 §11): a shell"] # [doc = " per type first (so recursive field references resolve), then its constructors. Duplicate type"] # [doc = " parameters / duplicate type names are explicit refusals. Extracted (M-662) so the phylum can build"] # [doc = " its registries once and reuse them; behavior is byte-identical to the inlined Pass 1."] pub (crate) fn register_types (types : & mut BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > , nodule : & Nodule ,) -> Result < () , CheckError > { for arity in collect_tuple_arities (nodule) { let tname = tuple_type_name (arity) ; types . entry (tname) . or_insert_with (| | synthetic_tuple_data (arity)) ; } for item in & nodule . items { if let Item :: Type (td) = item { if let Some (dup) = first_duplicate (& td . params) { return Err (CheckError :: new (& td . name , format ! ("duplicate type parameter `{dup}` in `{}`" , td . name) ,)) ; } if types . contains_key (& td . name) { return Err (CheckError :: new (& td . name , "duplicate type declaration")) ; } types . insert (td . name . clone () , DataInfo { name : td . name . clone () , home : nodule_home (& nodule . path) , params : td . params . clone () , ctors : vec ! [] , } ,) ; } } for item in & nodule . items { if let Item :: Type (td) = item { let ctors = resolve_ctors (types , td) ? ; types . get_mut (& td . name) . expect ("registered above") . ctors = ctors ; } } Ok (()) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Structural lowering of an `object` declaration (DN-53 §A.3.2, M-811): expand to the equivalent"] # [doc = " `Item::Type` + `Item::Impl` + `Item::Fn` items that the existing registration and checking passes"] # [doc = " already handle."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `via` delegation impls are **not** generated here — they require the trait registry (not yet"] # [doc = " built at the point this is called). They are generated by [`expand_object_via_decls`] and injected"] # [doc = " as additional `Item::Impl` items before the instance-registration and method-body passes."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Declared` (structural one-to-one rewrite; injective by construction — two distinct"] # [doc = " `object` declarations never produce the same item set, and the set determines the source uniquely;"] # [doc = " DN-53 §A.3.3). The three-way differential (`tests/object_desugar.rs`) provides `Empirical`"] # [doc = " agreement between the `object` form and its hand-written lowering."] fn desugar_object_structural (od : & ObjectDecl) -> Vec < Item > { let type_decl = TypeDecl { vis : od . vis , name : od . name . clone () , params : od . params . clone () , ctors : vec ! [od . ctor . clone ()] , } ; let mut items : Vec < Item > = vec ! [Item :: Type (type_decl)] ; for impl_decl in & od . impls { items . push (Item :: Impl (impl_decl . clone ())) ; } for fn_decl in & od . fns { items . push (Item :: Fn (fn_decl . clone ())) ; } items }

# [doc = " Generate forwarding `ImplDecl`s for each `via` clause of an `object` declaration"] # [doc = " (DN-53 §A.3.2, M-811). Called **after** `register_traits` so the trait's method signatures are"] # [doc = " available."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " For each `via <field_idx> : TraitName[args]`, generates an `impl TraitName for ObjectName` whose"] # [doc = " methods each destructure the object via a `match` to extract the delegate field, then forward the"] # [doc = " call. The delegate must implement the trait; the type-checker verifies this when it resolves the"] # [doc = " forwarding call."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Empirical` — the forwarding bodies are synthesized from the trait signatures; the"] # [doc = " three-way differential (`tests/object_desugar.rs`) pins the agreement"] # [doc = " `observe(object) == observe(lower(object))` (DN-53 §A.3.3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Never-silent (G2): an unknown trait name, an out-of-range field index, or **two `via` clauses on"] # [doc = " the same object delegating the same trait** (M-966 — an ambiguous choice between two delegates"] # [doc = " with no deterministic tiebreak) is an explicit [`CheckError`], never a silent accept or a"] # [doc = " silent first-match pick."] fn expand_object_via_decls (od : & ObjectDecl , traits : & BTreeMap < String , TraitInfo > ,) -> Result < Vec < ImplDecl > , CheckError > { let mut first_via_for_trait : BTreeMap < & str , u32 > = BTreeMap :: new () ; for via in & od . via_decls { if let Some (& first_idx) = first_via_for_trait . get (via . trait_name . as_str ()) { return Err (CheckError :: new (& od . name , format ! ("`object {}`: ambiguous `via` delegation for trait `{}` — both `via {} : {}` \
                     and `via {} : {}` claim to provide it, and there is no deterministic tiebreak \
                     between two delegates for the same trait (DN-53 §A.3.2; M-966). Fix: keep only \
                     one `via` clause per trait on this object, or replace one of them with an \
                     explicit `impl {} for {}` that picks the winner by hand — never a silent \
                     first-match (G2)." , od . name , via . trait_name , first_idx , via . trait_name , via . field_idx , via . trait_name , via . trait_name , od . name) ,)) ; } first_via_for_trait . insert (via . trait_name . as_str () , via . field_idx) ; } let mut via_impls : Vec < ImplDecl > = Vec :: new () ; for via in & od . via_decls { let trait_info = traits . get (& via . trait_name) . ok_or_else (| | { CheckError :: new (& od . name , format ! ("`object {}`: `via {} : {}` — trait `{}` is not declared in scope \
                     (DN-53 §A.3.2; `via` requires the trait to be visible — never a silent miss, G2)" , od . name , via . field_idx , via . trait_name , via . trait_name) ,) }) ? ; let n_fields = od . ctor . fields . len () ; if via . field_idx as usize >= n_fields { return Err (CheckError :: new (& od . name , format ! ("`object {}`: `via {} : {}` — field index {} is out of range; the constructor \
                     `{}` has {} field(s) (0-based; DN-53 §A.3.2; never silent — G2)" , od . name , via . field_idx , via . trait_name , via . field_idx , od . ctor . name , n_fields) ,)) ; } let obj_ty = TypeRef :: unguaranteed (if od . params . is_empty () { BaseType :: Named (od . name . clone () , vec ! []) } else { BaseType :: Named (od . name . clone () , od . params . iter () . map (| p | TypeRef :: unguaranteed (BaseType :: Named (p . clone () , vec ! []))) . collect () ,) }) ; let binders : Vec < String > = (0 .. n_fields) . map (| i | format ! ("_f{i}")) . collect () ; let delegate_var = binders [via . field_idx as usize] . clone () ; let match_pattern = Pattern :: Ctor (od . ctor . name . clone () , binders . iter () . map (| b | Pattern :: Ident (b . clone ())) . collect () ,) ; let mut methods : Vec < FnDecl > = Vec :: new () ; for sig in & trait_info . sigs { let sig = trait_info . params . iter () . zip (via . trait_args . iter ()) . fold (sig . clone () , | acc , (param , concrete) | { subst_type_param_in_sig (& acc , param , concrete) }) ; let self_name = sig . value_params . first () . map (| p | p . name . clone ()) . unwrap_or_else (| | "self" . to_string ()) ; let delegate_expr = Expr :: Match { scrutinee : Box :: new (Expr :: Path (Path (vec ! [self_name]))) , arms : vec ! [Arm { pattern : match_pattern . clone () , body : Expr :: Path (Path (vec ! [delegate_var . clone ()])) , }] , } ; let mut call_args : Vec < Expr > = vec ! [delegate_expr] ; for vp in sig . value_params . iter () . skip (1) { call_args . push (Expr :: Path (Path (vec ! [vp . name . clone ()]))) ; } let body = Expr :: App { head : Box :: new (Expr :: Path (Path (vec ! [sig . name . clone ()]))) , args : call_args , } ; methods . push (FnDecl { vis : crate :: ast :: Vis :: Private , thaw : false , tier : None , sig , body , }) ; } via_impls . push (ImplDecl { trait_name : via . trait_name . clone () , trait_args : via . trait_args . clone () , for_ty : obj_ty , methods , }) ; } Ok (via_impls) }

# [doc = " The core checker for **one nodule of a phylum** (M-662), run on an already ambient-resolved nodule"] # [doc = " with its pre-built declaration registries (`regs`), its resolved cross-nodule `imports`, the"] # [doc = " phylum-wide pub-blind `coherence` view, and an explicit maturation flag. When `matured_scope` is"] # [doc = " true, every fn with `thaw == false` must be `Total` (RFC-0017 §4.2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The nodule's checking registries = **its own declarations** merged with the **imported `pub`"] # [doc = " declarations** (own decls take precedence — the documented shadowing). The orphan rule is enforced"] # [doc = " **phylum-wide** (via `coherence`); name resolution is **never-silent** (an ambiguous glob name is"] # [doc = " refused at use-site). A phylum-of-one passes empty imports + a coherence view of just this nodule,"] # [doc = " so this reduces *exactly* to the pre-M-662 single-nodule checker."] fn check_nodule_with (nodule : & Nodule , regs : NoduleRegs , imports : & NoduleImports , coherence : & CoherenceView , matured_scope : bool , via_objects : & [ObjectDecl] ,) -> Result < Env , CheckError > { let mut types = imports . types . clone () ; types . extend (regs . types . clone ()) ; let mut traits = imports . traits . clone () ; traits . extend (regs . traits . clone ()) ; let mut fns = imports . fns . clone () ; fns . extend (regs . fns . clone ()) ; let via_generated_impls : Vec < ImplDecl > = { let mut all = Vec :: new () ; for od in via_objects { all . extend (expand_object_via_decls (od , & traits) ?) ; } all } ; let via_provenance : BTreeMap < (String , String) , (u32 , String) > = { let mut all = BTreeMap :: new () ; let this_home = nodule_home (& nodule . path) ; for od in via_objects { let head = format ! ("Data:{}" , qualify_type_name (& this_home , & od . name)) ; for via in & od . via_decls { all . insert ((via . trait_name . clone () , head . clone ()) , (via . field_idx , od . name . clone ()) ,) ; } } all } ; let mut lower_rules : BTreeMap < String , LowerDecl > = BTreeMap :: new () ; for item in & nodule . items { if let Item :: Lower (ld) = item { check_lower_decl_structural (ld , & lower_rules) ? ; lower_rules . insert (ld . name . clone () , ld . clone ()) ; } } check_lower_rule_acyclicity (& lower_rules , & types , & fns) ? ; let mut derive_generated_impls : Vec < ImplDecl > = Vec :: new () ; let mut derived_provenance : BTreeMap < (String , String) , (String , String) > = BTreeMap :: new () ; let mut derived_seen : BTreeMap < (String , String) , ImplDecl > = BTreeMap :: new () ; for item in & nodule . items { let Item :: Derive (dd) = item else { continue } ; check_derive_application (dd , & lower_rules , & types) ? ; let rule = & lower_rules [& dd . name] ; let Some (template) = rule . impl_rhs () else { continue ; } ; if rule . params . len () != 1 { return Err (CheckError :: new (& dd . name , format ! ("`derive {} for …` targets an item-shaped rule with {} type parameter(s), but \
                     Model-A sibling injection binds the single `derive` target type to exactly one \
                     rule parameter (`lower {}[T] = impl … for T`; DN-54 §10.3 / OQ-C multi-param is \
                     a future extension — never a silent mis-instantiation, G2)" , dd . name , rule . params . len () , dd . name) ,)) ; } let concrete = subst_type_param_in_impl (template , & rule . params [0] , & dd . for_ty) ; let (for_ty_resolved , _) = resolve_ty (& dd . name , & types , & [] , & concrete . for_ty) ? ; let Some (head) = type_head (& for_ty_resolved) else { return Err (CheckError :: new (& dd . name , format ! ("`derive {} for {for_ty_resolved}` instantiates to an `impl … for \
                     {for_ty_resolved}` whose target is not a concrete instance head (DN-54 §10.2 / \
                     RFC-0019 §4.5 — a derive target must be a concrete type, never a bare type \
                     variable; G2)" , dd . name) ,)) ; } ; let key = (concrete . trait_name . clone () , head) ; match derived_seen . get (& key) { Some (prev) if * prev == concrete => continue , _ => { } } derived_seen . insert (key . clone () , concrete . clone ()) ; derived_provenance . insert (key , (dd . name . clone () , format ! ("{for_ty_resolved}"))) ; derive_generated_impls . push (concrete) ; } let effective_nodule_owned : Option < Nodule > = if via_generated_impls . is_empty () && derive_generated_impls . is_empty () { None } else { let mut items = nodule . items . clone () ; for id in & via_generated_impls { items . push (Item :: Impl (id . clone ())) ; } for id in & derive_generated_impls { items . push (Item :: Impl (id . clone ())) ; } Some (Nodule { path : nodule . path . clone () , std_sys : nodule . std_sys , items , }) } ; let effective_nodule : & Nodule = effective_nodule_owned . as_ref () . unwrap_or (nodule) ; let instances = register_instances (& types , & traits , coherence , effective_nodule) ? ; let mut resolved_fns : BTreeMap < String , FnDecl > = fns . clone () ; for fd in regs . fns . values () { let (body , _ret) = check_fn_body (& types , & fns , & traits , & instances , imports , & lower_rules , nodule . std_sys , fd ,) ? ; resolved_fns . insert (fd . sig . name . clone () , FnDecl { vis : fd . vis , thaw : fd . thaw , tier : fd . tier , sig : fd . sig . clone () , body , } ,) ; } let fns = resolved_fns ; let mut resolved_impl_methods : Vec < FnDecl > = Vec :: new () ; let mut impls : BTreeMap < (String , String) , Vec < FnDecl > > = BTreeMap :: new () ; for item in & effective_nodule . items { if let Item :: Impl (id) = item { let methods = check_impl_methods (& types , & fns , & traits , & instances , imports , & lower_rules , effective_nodule . std_sys , id ,) ? ; let (for_ty , _) = resolve_ty (& id . trait_name , & types , & [] , & id . for_ty) ? ; let head = type_head (& for_ty) . expect ("instance registration refused a non-concrete `for` type head") ; impls . insert ((id . trait_name . clone () , head) , methods . clone ()) ; resolved_impl_methods . extend (methods) ; } } crate :: fuse :: check_fuse_laws (& types , & fns , & traits , & instances , & impls) ? ; for ld in lower_rules . values () { check_lower_rule_rhs_type (ld , & types , & fns , & traits , & instances , imports , & lower_rules) ? ; } check_effect_coverage (& fns , & regs . fns , & traits , effective_nodule) ? ; crate :: grade :: check_guarantees (& fns , & regs . fns , & resolved_impl_methods) ? ; let totality = crate :: totality :: classify_all (& fns) . map_err (| e | CheckError :: new (& qualify (& nodule . path , "<totality>") , e . to_string ())) ? ; if matured_scope { for fd in regs . fns . values () { if ! fd . thaw && totality [& fd . sig . name] != crate :: totality :: Totality :: Total { return Err (CheckError :: new (& fd . sig . name , format ! ("`{}` is in a matured scope and must be total (RFC-0007 §4.5 / \
                         RFC-0017 §4.2) — mark it `thaw fn` to exempt it, or make it total" , fd . sig . name) ,)) ; } } } Ok (Env { types , fns , totality , traits , instances , impls , lower_rules , derived_provenance , via_provenance , }) }

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " **Structural** validation of a `lower Name[params] = <rhs>` declaration (DN-54 §3/§4.6 /"] # [doc = " M-812-cont) — the part that does **not** need the full typing context. Run in the registration"] # [doc = " loop *before* acyclicity (§4.2) and the §4.1 RHS type-check (which run over the whole set)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee: `Declared`** (structural checks, not theorems). Checks (all never-silent, G2):"] # [doc = " - **Uniqueness**: the rule `name` must not already be registered."] # [doc = " - **Parameter uniqueness**: duplicate param names in `[…]` are refused."] # [doc = " - **§4.6 purity**: the RHS contains no `wild { … }` block — a generative lowering is a *pure*"] # [doc = "   compile-time mechanism (the FFI gate is level-independent — DN-38 §3), refused structurally so"] # [doc = "   the refusal holds even in an `@std-sys` nodule (where the ordinary `wild` gate would accept it)."] fn check_lower_decl_structural (ld : & LowerDecl , lower_rules : & BTreeMap < String , LowerDecl > ,) -> Result < () , CheckError > { if lower_rules . contains_key (& ld . name) { return Err (CheckError :: new (& ld . name , format ! ("duplicate `lower` rule `{}` — a generative-lowering rule name must be unique \
                 within a nodule (DN-54 §4.2 / M-812; never silent, G2)" , ld . name) ,)) ; } if let Some (dup) = first_duplicate (& ld . params) { return Err (CheckError :: new (& ld . name , format ! ("duplicate type parameter `{dup}` in `lower {}[…]` (DN-54 §3 / M-812; \
                 every param name must be distinct)" , ld . name) ,)) ; } let has_wild = match & ld . rhs { crate :: ast :: LowerRhs :: Expr (e) => { rhs_contains_wild (e) . map_err (| err | CheckError :: new (& ld . name , err . to_string ())) ? } crate :: ast :: LowerRhs :: Impl (id) => { let mut found = false ; for m in & id . methods { if rhs_contains_wild (& m . body) . map_err (| err | CheckError :: new (& ld . name , err . to_string ())) ? { found = true ; break ; } } found } } ; if has_wild { return Err (CheckError :: new (& ld . name , format ! ("`lower {}`'s RHS contains a `wild {{ … }}` block — a generative-lowering rule is a \
                 pure compile-time mechanism and may not perform host/FFI calls (DN-54 §4.6 / \
                 §3.3; the `wild` gate is level-independent — DN-38 §3; never silent, G2)" , ld . name) ,)) ; } Ok (()) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **Effect-coverage pass** (RFC-0014 §3.4/§4.5 I3; M-660 — guarantee: `Declared`). For every"] # [doc = " top-level function **and every `impl`-method body**, the effects it **performs** must be a subset"] # [doc = " of the effects it **declares**. The *performed* set is the union, over every call in the body, of"] # [doc = " the declared effects of the callee — a **known top-level fn** `g` (in `fns`) OR an unqualified"] # [doc = " **trait-method** call (the declaring trait method's effects, from `traits`; an ambiguous method"] # [doc = " name was already refused at the call site — M-659, so the name maps to one trait in any program"] # [doc = " reaching here). This is the v0 \"manual-declare + compositional-check\" line (RFC-0014 §8): the"] # [doc = " checker *composes* declared effects up the call graph as a **check**, never *infers* an undeclared"] # [doc = " one. gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **MED closure (2026-07-11, adversarial-verification follow-up to the CRITICAL ctor-field fix"] # [doc = " above):** a **foreign** (cross-phylum-imported) trait's method signatures are surface"] # [doc = " [`TypeRef`]s, resolved **lazily** — at `impl`-check time, in [`check_impl_methods`]'s"] # [doc = " `resolve_ty(site, types, &tr.params, &rp.ty)` — against the IMPLEMENTING phylum's OWN type"] # [doc = " registry. They are never re-homed against the trait's own declaring phylum the way a ctor field"] # [doc = " now is (the CRITICAL fix in [`merge_phyla_exports`]) or a fn-sig always was"] # [doc = " (`Exports::resolved_fn_sigs`). gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **HOLE B closure (M-1060 fix-cycle-3, 2026-07-11, adversarial-verification 3rd-cycle finding):**"] # [doc = " the fn-signature analogue of [`foreign_trait_sig_names_a_concrete_type`] — does a **foreign**"] # [doc = " (cross-phylum-imported) fn's OWN signature mention a concrete named type beyond its own generic"] # [doc = " type parameters, when [`NoduleImports::resolved_fn_sigs`] has **no baked entry** for it (the"] # [doc = " un-bakeable case: [`resolve_fn_sig`] only resolves against the declaring nodule's own registered"] # [doc = " types, so a signature that itself references a type the declaring nodule imports from a sibling"] # [doc = " nodule fails to bake — see that fn's doc comment)? # [doc = " **Impl pass — registration + coherence** (RFC-0019 §4.5; LR-2 — guarantee: `Declared`, the"] # [doc = " coherence argument is Declared-with-argument per RFC-0019, not machine-checked). For each"] # [doc = " `impl Trait<args> for T`:"] # [doc = " - resolve `for_ty` and each `trait_args` to a **concrete** [`Ty`] (no type-variables in scope);"] # [doc = " - the trait must exist and the argument **arity** must match the trait's params (else explicit);"] # [doc = " - **global uniqueness:** key `(trait, type_head(for_ty))` must be free, else an overlapping-"] # [doc = "   instance / coherence refusal naming the pair (RFC-0019 §4.5; ADR-003);"] # [doc = " - **orphan rule (phylum-wide, pub-blind — M-662):** the trait is declared in *some* nodule of the"] # [doc = "   phylum, OR `for_ty`'s head is a `Data` declared in *some* nodule of the phylum, OR `for_ty` is a"] # [doc = "   primitive repr type (`Binary`/`Ternary`/`Dense`/`Substrate`); otherwise an explicit orphan"] # [doc = "   refusal. The locality view is pub-blind (coherence is enforcement authority, not the `pub`"] # [doc = "   namespace — RFC-0019 §4.5). For a phylum-of-one this reduces to the prior single-nodule test."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Method *bodies* are not checked here (that is [`check_impl_methods`], after the whole instance"] # [doc = " set is known); method *presence* (exact-match against the trait's sigs) IS checked here."] pub (crate) fn register_instances (types : & BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > , traits : & BTreeMap < String , TraitInfo > , coherence : & CoherenceView , nodule : & Nodule ,) -> Result < BTreeMap < (String , String) , InstanceInfo > , CheckError > { let phylum_traits = & coherence . traits ; let phylum_types = & coherence . types ; let mut instances : BTreeMap < (String , String) , InstanceInfo > = BTreeMap :: new () ; for item in & nodule . items { let Item :: Impl (id) = item else { continue } ; let site = & id . trait_name ; let Some (tr) = traits . get (& id . trait_name) else { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("`impl` for unknown trait `{}` (RFC-0019 §4.5)" , id . trait_name) ,)) ; } ; let (for_ty , _) = resolve_ty (site , types , & [] , & id . for_ty) ? ; let mut trait_args = Vec :: with_capacity (id . trait_args . len ()) ; for a in & id . trait_args { trait_args . push (resolve_ty (site , types , & [] , a) ? . 0) ; } if trait_args . len () != tr . params . len () { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("trait `{}` takes {} type argument(s), but this `impl` supplies {} \
                     (RFC-0019 §4.5)" , tr . name , tr . params . len () , trait_args . len ()) ,)) ; } let Some (head) = type_head (& for_ty) else { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("an `impl … for {for_ty}` over a bare type variable is not a legal instance \
                     head in stage-1 (no blanket instances — RFC-0019 §4.5); the `for` type must \
                     be concrete") ,)) ; } ; let trait_local = phylum_traits . contains (id . trait_name . as_str ()) ; let type_local = match & for_ty { Ty :: Data (n , _) => phylum_types . contains (ty_local_name (n)) , Ty :: Binary (_) | Ty :: Ternary (_) | Ty :: Dense (_ , _) | Ty :: Vsa { .. } | Ty :: Substrate (_) | Ty :: Seq (_ , _) | Ty :: Bytes | Ty :: Float => true , Ty :: Var (_) | Ty :: Fn (_ , _) => false , } ; if ! trait_local && ! type_local { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("orphan instance: `impl {} for {for_ty}` — neither the trait `{}` nor the type \
                     `{for_ty}` is declared in any nodule of this phylum (RFC-0019 §4.5 orphan rule, \
                     phylum-wide; M-662). Declare one of them in the phylum, or move the impl." , id . trait_name , id . trait_name) ,)) ; } if ! trait_local { if let Some (name) = foreign_trait_sig_names_a_concrete_type (tr) { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("`impl {} for {for_ty}` — the foreign (cross-phylum) trait `{}`'s \
                         signature mentions a concrete type `{name}` beyond its own generic \
                         parameter(s); a cross-phylum trait's signature is not yet re-homed \
                         against its declaring phylum (DN-113 §7 / DN-122 residual — a bare-name \

                         level up; refused rather than silently resolved against a possibly-wrong \

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The impl's method **set** must match the trait's requirement set **exactly** (RFC-0019 §4.5):"] # [doc = " a missing method and an extra method are both explicit refusals (never silently filled or"] # [doc = " dropped — G2). Signature/body agreement is [`check_impl_methods`]; this is only presence/names."] fn check_impl_method_set (tr : & TraitInfo , id : & ImplDecl) -> Result < () , CheckError > { let site = & id . trait_name ; let required : std :: collections :: BTreeSet < & str > = tr . sigs . iter () . map (| s | s . name . as_str ()) . collect () ; let provided : std :: collections :: BTreeSet < & str > = id . methods . iter () . map (| m | m . sig . name . as_str ()) . collect () ; for need in & required { if ! provided . contains (need) { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("impl of `{}` is missing method `{need}`" , tr . name) ,)) ; } } for have in & provided { if ! required . contains (have) { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("impl of `{}` has method `{have}` not in trait `{}`" , tr . name , tr . name) ,)) ; } } if let Some (dup) = first_duplicate (& id . methods . iter () . map (| m | m . sig . name . clone ()) . collect :: < Vec < _ > > () ,) { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("impl of `{}` provides method `{dup}` more than once" , tr . name) ,)) ; } Ok (()) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Check a function (or impl method) body against its declared signature (RFC-0007 §11; RFC-0019"] # [doc = " §4.1). Validates the type-parameter bounds, builds the `tyvars`/`bounds` scopes, resolves the"] # [doc = " value-param + return types, and runs the bidirectional [`Cx::check`]. Returns the **resolved**"] # [doc = " body (ambient bare-decimals filled) and the resolved return type. Shared by Pass 3 (top-level"] # [doc = " fns) and [`check_impl_methods`] (impl methods) — DRY."] # [allow (clippy :: too_many_arguments)] fn check_fn_body (types : & BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > , fns : & BTreeMap < String , FnDecl > , traits : & BTreeMap < String , TraitInfo > , instances : & BTreeMap < (String , String) , InstanceInfo > , imports : & NoduleImports , lower_rules : & BTreeMap < String , LowerDecl > , std_sys : bool , fd : & FnDecl ,) -> Result < (Expr , Ty) , CheckError > { let site = & fd . sig . name ; let tyvars = fd . sig . param_names () ; let bounds = check_bounds (types , traits , site , & tyvars , & fd . sig . params) ? ; let mut scope : Vec < (String , Ty) > = Vec :: new () ; for p in & fd . sig . value_params { let (ty , _) = resolve_ty (site , types , & tyvars , & p . ty) ? ; scope . push ((p . name . clone () , ty)) ; } let (ret , _) = resolve_ty (site , types , & tyvars , & fd . sig . ret) ? ; let cx = Cx { site , types , fns , traits , instances , imports , lower_rules , tyvars : & tyvars , bounds : & bounds , std_sys , depth : Cell :: new (0) , affine : Tracker :: seeded (& scope) , # [cfg (test)] stage3_sabotage_skip_substitution : false , } ; let (got , body) = cx . check (& mut scope , & fd . body , Some (& ret)) ? ; if got != ret { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , edge_mismatch ("body" , & ret , & got))) ; } Ok ((body , ret)) }

# [doc = " Validate a function/method's type-parameter **bounds** (RFC-0019 §4.1): each bound must name a"] # [doc = " registered trait with the correct type-argument arity, and the bound's trait-args must resolve"] # [doc = " under `tyvars`. Returns the `(param, bounds)` pairs (only the bounded params) for the checking"] # [doc = " context. Every refusal is explicit (G2). The dictionary the bound stands for is staged to"] # [doc = " elaboration (RFC-0007 §12.3 / M-673) — the checker only validates satisfiability (\"typing\")."] fn check_bounds (types : & BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > , traits : & BTreeMap < String , TraitInfo > , site : & str , tyvars : & [String] , params : & [crate :: ast :: TypeParam] ,) -> Result < Vec < (String , Vec < TraitRef >) > , CheckError > { let mut bounds : Vec < (String , Vec < TraitRef >) > = Vec :: new () ; for p in params { for b in & p . bounds { let Some (tr) = traits . get (& b . name) else { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("bound `{}: {}` names unknown trait `{}` (RFC-0019 §4.1)" , p . name , b . name , b . name) ,)) ; } ; let elided_self = b . args . is_empty () && tr . params . len () == 1 ; if ! elided_self && b . args . len () != tr . params . len () { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format !                          (write `{}: {}` for the single-parameter self-bound, or supply all args)" , p . name , b . name , b . args . len () , b . name , tr . params . len () , p . name , b . name) ,)) ; } for a in & b . args { resolve_ty (site , types , tyvars , a) ? ; } } if ! p . bounds . is_empty () { bounds . push ((p . name . clone () , p . bounds . clone ())) ; } } Ok (bounds) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The checking context for one function body."] struct Cx < 'a > { site : & 'a str , types : & 'a BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > , fns : & 'a BTreeMap < String , FnDecl > , # [doc = " Trait registry (RFC-0019 §4.2) — for resolving bounded-generic-call satisfiability and"] # [doc = " unqualified trait-method calls (`Tr::m`). Empty in re-inference (`infer_type`)."] traits : & 'a BTreeMap < String , TraitInfo > , # [doc = " Instance registry (RFC-0019 §4.5), keyed by `(trait, type-head)` — the coherence map a"] # [doc = " bounded call / trait-method call resolves against. Empty in re-inference."] instances : & 'a BTreeMap < (String , String) , InstanceInfo > , # [doc = " The nodule's resolved cross-nodule imports (M-662) — consulted **only** at unresolved-name"] # [doc = " sites to raise the never-silent glob-vs-glob ambiguity error (a *reference* to a name brought"] # [doc = " in by ≥2 globs and not shadowed). Imported `pub` decls themselves are already merged into"] # [doc = " `types`/`fns`/`traits` (by simple name), so ordinary resolution sees them directly; this field"] # [doc = " only carries the `ambiguous` set so a reference to an ambiguous name is refused, never a silent"] # [doc = " winner (G2). Empty (`ambiguous` empty) in re-inference and in a phylum-of-one. **Also**"] # [doc = " carries [`NoduleImports::resolved_fn_sigs`] (DN-112 Rank 1 / M-1036) — [`Self::check_app`]'s"] # [doc = " monomorphic-callee path consults it in preference to re-resolving a callee's signature"] # [doc = " fresh against `self.types` (the caller's own registry), so an imported function's"] # [doc = " parameter/return types stay the *declaring* nodule's, never re-derived at the call site."] imports : & 'a NoduleImports , # [doc = " **M-1054 Stage 0** — the nodule's registered `lower` rules (DN-110 §5-A), consulted **only**"] # [doc = " after every existing call-resolution path (fn/HOF/ctor/trait-method/prim families) has"] # [doc = " already missed [`Self::check_app`], so a name that resolves any other way is completely"] # [doc = " unaffected by this field's presence (regression-safe by construction). Threaded down from the nodule header through [`check_fn_body`] / [`check_impl_methods`]."] std_sys : bool , # [doc = " Live expression-nesting depth for the explicit [`MAX_CHECK_DEPTH`] budget (interior"] # [doc = " mutability so [`Self::check`] stays `&self`). (FLAG-6 fix, M-973: this doc previously read that the"] # [doc = " tracker was inert in `check_lower_rule_rhs_type`, stale since M-919 made it active there.)"] affine : Tracker , # [doc = " **Test-only non-vacuity sabotage toggle (M-1054 Stage 3, DN-117 §5 item 2).** When `true`,"] # [doc = " [`Self::check_sugar_call`]'s Stage-3 walk feeds the walk the **unsubstituted** `rhs` instead"] # [doc = " of the real, spliced [`Self::stage3_substitute_expr`] output — simulating \"the substitution"] # [doc = " never ran\" to prove the substituted-`Expr` walk is load-bearing: a fixture that must"] # [doc = " genuinely REFUSE (e.g. the composite/hidden-affine classes no type-based gate alone can"] # [doc = " catch) should wrongly ACCEPT under this sabotage. **Does not exist in a non-test build at"] # [doc = " all** (`#[cfg(test)]`-gated field — zero production footprint); every non-test `Cx`"] # [doc = " construction site sets it `false` (also `#[cfg(test)]`-gated, so it only compiles under"] # [doc = " `cfg(test)` too — see each `Cx { .. }` literal). gaps.reason: lifetime parameter `'a` has no grammar surface

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " RFC-0040 (M-977): if `expected` is a **cons-list-shaped** user ADT, return its `(nil, cons)`"] # [doc = " constructor names — the trigger for desugaring a `[…]` literal into a right-nested `Cons` chain"] # [doc = " (see [`Cx::check_list`]). \"Cons-list-shaped\" is a purely *structural* recognition, requiring no"] # [doc = " annotation: the type is a `Ty::Data(name, _)` whose declaration has **exactly two** constructors —"] # [doc = " one **nullary** (the \"nil\": `Nil`/`GLNil`/`TNil`/`BNil`/…) and one **binary** whose SECOND field is"] # [doc = " the recursive `Self` type `Data(name, …)` (the \"cons\": `Cons(A, Self)`). This matches every"] # [doc = " `lib/std` list type (`Vec`, `Trits`, `ByteList`, `GRowList`, …) uniformly. A type not of this shape"] # [doc = " yields `None`, so the `Seq{T,N}` and no-context paths are untouched (never a silent reinterpret)."] pub (crate) fn cons_list_ctors (types : & std :: collections :: BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > , expected : & Ty ,) -> Option < (String , String) > { let Ty :: Data (name , _args) = expected else { return None ; } ; let di = lookup_data (types , name) ? ; if di . ctors . len () != 2 { return None ; } let mut nil : Option < String > = None ; let mut cons : Option < String > = None ; for c in & di . ctors { match c . fields . len () { 0 => nil = Some (c . name . clone ()) , 2 => { if let Ty :: Data (fname , fargs) = & c . fields [1] { if fname == name && fargs . len () == di . params . len () { cons = Some (c . name . clone ()) ; } } } _ => { } } } match (nil , cons) { (Some (n) , Some (c)) => Some ((n , c)) , _ => None , } }
gaps.reason: qualified type path `std :: collections :: BTreeMap < String , DataInfo >` — collapsing to its last segment would risk colliding with an unrelated same-named local type (e.g. `std::cmp::Ordering` vs a local `Ordering`); left an explicit gap rather than guessed (VR-5)

impl Cx < '_ > { fn err < T > (& self , msg : impl Into < String >) -> Result < T , CheckError > { Err (CheckError :: new (self . site , msg)) } # [doc = " Map a match-analysis over-budget refusal (usefulness / decision-tree, RFC-0041 §4.7) into the"] # [doc = " checker's never-silent [`CheckError`] surface — an explicit refusal, never a host-stack"] # [doc = " overflow (G2/VR-5). Shared by the exhaustiveness/redundancy (`usefulness::useful`) and the"] # [doc = " decision-tree (`decision::compile`) callsites so the mapping is written once (DRY)."] fn match_budget_err (& self , e : mycelium_workstack :: BudgetError) -> CheckError { CheckError :: new (self . site , format ! ("match analysis exceeded the recursion budget: {e} — an explicit over-budget refusal \
                 (RFC-0041 §4.7), never a host-stack overflow (G2/VR-5)") ,) } # [doc = " Enter one level of `check` recursion against the explicit [`MAX_CHECK_DEPTH`] budget"] # [doc = " (banked guard 4): charge a level, refuse with a clean [`CheckError`] past the budget (never a"] # [doc = " host-stack overflow), and return a [`DepthGuard`] that releases the level on **any** exit path."] fn enter (& self) -> Result < DepthGuard < '_ > , CheckError > { let d = self . depth . get () + 1 ; if d > MAX_CHECK_DEPTH { return self . err (format ! ("expression nesting exceeds the checker depth budget ({MAX_CHECK_DEPTH}) — an \
                 explicit budget (banked guard 4), refused cleanly rather than overflowing the \
                 host stack (RFC-0007 §4.6 clocked-recursion discipline)")) ; } self . depth . set (d) ; Ok (DepthGuard (& self . depth)) } fn ctor (& self , name : & str) -> Option < (& DataInfo , usize) > { self . types . values () . find_map (| d | d . ctors . iter () . position (| c | c . name == name) . map (| i | (d , i))) } # [doc = " M-1027 / DN-104 §4: the **cross-nodule construction seal**. If `name` is a constructor **imported"] # [doc = " by name** (`use home.Ctor`) from another nodule where it is `priv`-sealed, **constructing** it"] # [doc = " here is a never-silent [`CheckError`]. Returns `Err` at the two constructor-application sites for"] # [doc = " a withheld name; `Ok(())` otherwise (an unsealed ctor, or a home constructor — own decls are"] # [doc = " subtracted from the withheld set in `resolve_imports`). The type **name** and **pattern-matching**"] # [doc = " are unaffected — only *construction* is withheld (DN-104 §4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Not an enforced security/capability boundary (known gap, M-1036)** — this check is keyed by"] # [doc = " bare constructor name in [`NoduleImports::sealed`], so a caller that declares its own same-named"] # [doc = " **local** (unsealed) type/ctor — rather than importing the real one — never populates its"] # [doc = " withheld set and bypasses this check entirely (own decls shadow imports, RFC-0006 §4.3; pinned"] # [doc = " by `tests/ctor_seal.rs::known_gap_a_same_named_local_shadow_type_bypasses_the_seal`). It refuses"] # [doc = " a well-behaved cross-nodule caller that actually goes through `use`; it does not defend against"] # [doc = " an adversarial or accidentally-colliding same name."] fn check_ctor_seal (& self , name : & str , ty_name : & str) -> Result < () , CheckError > { if self . imports . sealed . contains (name) { return self . err (format ! ("constructor `{name}` of `{ty_name}` is `priv`-sealed in its home nodule — it is \
                 exported for use in signatures and pattern-matching, but **withheld from \
                 cross-nodule construction** (the capability-gate: only its home nodule may mint one; \

                 home nodule exposes (never a silent forge — G2/VR-5)")) ; } Ok (()) } # [doc = " Infer the type of `e` under `scope` (a lexical stack; shadowing = later wins). A thin wrapper"] # [doc = " over the bidirectional [`Self::check`] with no expected type — used where only the type is"] # [doc = " wanted and `e` carries no ambient bare-decimal needing context (e.g. the elaborator's"] # [doc = " re-inference over already-resolved terms)."] fn infer (& self , scope : & mut Vec < (String , Ty) > , e : & Expr) -> Result < Ty , CheckError > { self . check (scope , e , None) . map (| (ty , _) | ty) } # [doc = " **Bidirectional check** (RFC-0012 §4.3): type `e` under `scope`, optionally *against* an"] # [doc = " `expected` type, and return the type together with a **resolved** expression — any ambient"] # [doc = " bare decimal ([`Literal::AmbientInt`]) whose width the context determines is rewritten to a"] # [doc = " concrete `Binary`/`Ternary` literal, so the evaluator and elaborator see only explicit forms."] # [doc = " Where the width is *not* determined, the refusal is an explicit `UnresolvedWidth` (never a"] # [doc = " built-in default). A cross-paradigm edge surfaces as a `MissingConversion` (never silent)."] fn check (& self , scope : & mut Vec < (String , Ty) > , e : & Expr , expected : Option < & Ty > ,) -> Result < (Ty , Expr) , CheckError > { let _depth = self . enter () ? ; match e { Expr :: Lit (Literal :: AmbientInt (p , v)) => { let lit = self . resolve_ambient_int (* p , * v , expected) ? ; let ty = lit_ty_of (self . site , & lit) ? ; Ok ((ty , Expr :: Lit (lit))) } Expr :: Lit (Literal :: List (elems)) => self . check_list (scope , elems , expected) , Expr :: Lit (l) => Ok ((self . lit_ty (l) ? , Expr :: Lit (l . clone ()))) , Expr :: Path (p) => self . check_path (scope , p , e , expected) , Expr :: Let { name , ty , bound , body , } => self . check_let (scope , name , ty . as_ref () , bound , body , expected) , Expr :: If { cond , conseq , alt } => self . check_if (scope , cond , conseq , alt , expected) , Expr :: Match { scrutinee , arms } => self . check_match (scope , scrutinee , arms , expected) , Expr :: For { x , xs , acc , init , body , } => self . check_for (scope , x , xs , acc , init , body , expected) , Expr :: Swap { value , target , policy , } => self . check_swap (scope , value , target , policy) , Expr :: Wild (body) => self . check_wild (body , expected) , Expr :: Spore (_) => { self . err ("`spore` is deferred to the reconstruction-manifest work (E2-5/M-260)") } Expr :: Wrapping (body) => self . check_wrapping (scope , body , expected) , Expr :: Consume (operand) => self . check_consume (scope , operand , expected) , Expr :: Try (_) => self . err ("`?` (the try-operator) is only supported as a `let`-binder RHS in v0 — write \

The"] # [doc = " operand must have a `Substrate{tag}` type; any other operand type is an explicit refusal"] # [doc = " (never silent — G2). The result is the moved substrate (`Substrate{tag}`), now exclusively"] # [doc = " owned by the consumer."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee **`Empirical`** (M-903; DN-71 Model S §4.2/§4.4 — updated from the M-664-era"] # [doc = " `Declared`, note that stated the pre-M-903 baseline honestly and is stale as of this"] # [doc = " affine-tracker landing, VR-5 requires this update rather than leaving it): the *single-use*"] # [doc = " property is now **checked**, not merely asserted — every reference to a `Substrate`-typed"] # [doc = " binding is recorded in `self.affine` (`crate::affine::Tracker`, wired below via `use_at` at"] # [doc = " every scope reference) and a second move on any reachable path is refused here, naming both"] # [doc = " sites. `Empirical`, not `Proven`: the tracker's own module docs are explicit that it is a"] # [doc = " sound-over-precise conservative approximation (verified by an exhaustive sweep, not a"] # [doc = " mechanized soundness proof) with a known, documented loop/closure multiplicity gap closed only"] # [doc = " by [`crate::substrate::SubstrateHandle::try_consume`]'s runtime backstop (M-903; wired into"] # [doc = " execution by M-904, DN-71 §4.3)."] fn check_consume (& self , scope : & mut Vec < (String , Ty) > , operand : & Expr , expected : Option < & Ty > ,) -> Result < (Ty , Expr) , CheckError > { let (oty , oexpr) = self . check (scope , operand , None) ? ; let Ty :: Substrate (tag) = & oty else { return self . err (format ! [] } else { match expected { Some (Ty :: Data (en , eargs)) if * en == qname && eargs . len () == d . params . len () => { eargs . clone () } _ => { return self . err (format ! ("constructor `{name}` of generic `{}<…>` needs its type \

[p . name . clone ()]))) . collect () , } ; let mut body : Expr = call ; for p in vparams . iter () . rev () { body = Expr :: Lambda { params : vec ! The `?` on the"] # [doc = " binder RHS propagates the operand's error/absence channel: it rewrites, **type-directed on the"] # [doc = " operand's checked `Result`/`Option` type**, to the existing `match` bind with the continuation"] # [doc = " `body` inside the binding arm —"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ```text"] # [doc = " let x = e? in body                       let x = e? in body"] # [doc = "   ⇓  (e : Result[A, E])                    ⇓  (e : Option[A])"] # [doc = " match e { Ok(x) => body,                 match e { Some(x) => body,"] # [doc = "           Err($try_err) => Err($try_err) }          None => None }"] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Because `body` is in tail position its type is the enclosing function's return type"] # [doc = " `Result[B, E]` (resp. `Option[B]`), so the `Err($f) => Err($f)` arm (`Result[B, E]`) **unifies**"] # [doc = " with it — **no early return, no never-type** (`->!` stays deferred, DN-99 #88; DN-102 §2). The"] # [doc = " **error-type unification rule** (DN-102 §3) falls out of that arm-type unification: `E` on both"] # [doc = " sides must match, forcing the function to return the same error channel — a mismatch is the"] # [doc = " ordinary never-silent `match`-arm `CheckError` (G2). Guarantee: `Declared` (surface contract)"] # [doc = " until the DN-102 §7 differential witnesses `?` ≡ the hand-`match` oracle (VR-5)."] fn check_try_let (& self , scope : & mut Vec < (String , Ty) > , name : & str , inner : & Expr , body : & Expr , expected : Option < & Ty > ,) -> Result < (Ty , Expr) , CheckError > { let affine_snap = self . affine . snapshot () ; let ity = self . infer (scope , inner) ? ; self . affine . restore (& affine_snap) ; const ERRB : & str = "$try_err" ; let arms = match & ity { Ty :: Data (n , args) if ty_local_name (n) == "Result" && args . len () == 2 => vec ! [Arm { pattern : Pattern :: Ctor ("Ok" . to_owned () , vec ! [Pattern :: Ident (name . to_owned ())]) , body : body . clone () , } , Arm { pattern : Pattern :: Ctor ("Err" . to_owned () , vec ! The static shape is width-preserving exactly as the non-wrapping"] # [doc = " `bin.add`/`bin.sub`/`bin.mul` (the modular semantics is a *runtime* mode, not a type change)."] fn check_wrapping (& self , scope : & mut Vec < (String , Ty) > , body : & Expr , expected : Option < & Ty > ,) -> Result < (Ty , Expr) , CheckError > { self . gate_wrapping_body (body) ? ; let (ty , rbody) = self . check (scope , body , expected) ? ; Ok ((ty , Expr :: Wrapping (Box :: new (rbody)))) } # [doc = " The structural gate for a `wrapping { … }` body (RFC-0034 §10.1): accept only a `Binary`"] # [doc = " add/sub/mul arithmetic tree — internal nodes are single-segment applications whose"] # [doc = " [`prim_kernel_name`] is `bin.add`/`bin.sub`/`bin.mul` (arity 2), leaves are `Path`/`Lit`"] # [doc = " operands — and refuse everything else with an explicit, teaching refusal (never-silent — G2)."] # [doc = " The operand types (`Binary`, equal width) are *not* checked here — that is the normal-typing"] # [doc = " pass's job ([`prim_sig`]); this gate only constrains the *shape* so the region is a call-free"] # [doc = " arithmetic tree (see [`Self::check_wrapping`])."] fn gate_wrapping_body (& self , e : & Expr) -> Result < () , CheckError > { match e { Expr :: Path (_) | Expr :: Lit (_) => Ok (()) , Expr :: App { head , args } => { if let Expr :: Path (p) = head . as_ref () { if p . 0 . len () == 1 && args . len () == 2 && matches ! (prim_kernel_name (& p . 0 [0]) , Some ("bin.add" | "bin.sub" | "bin.mul")) { self . gate_wrapping_body (& args [0]) ? ; return self . gate_wrapping_body (& args [1]) ; } } self . err ("a `wrapping { … }` block accepts only an enclosed `Binary` add/sub/mul tree \

Returns `Ok(None)` if `name` is not one of them."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Model + dim + sparsity live in the type (`VSA{model, dim, sparsity}`), so the static rules"] # [doc = " are: every hypervector operand must be a `VSA` type with **one shared model + dim +"] # [doc = " sparsity** (a mismatch is an explicit refusal, never a coercion — G2); the model must be"] # [doc = " in the **introduction dispatch set MAP-I / FHRR / BSC** (an out-of-set model — HRR/MAP-B/"] # [doc = " SBC mentions included — is a static refusal naming the set; widening is append-only and"] # [doc = " mirrors the interpreter's dispatch); and the sparsity must be `Dense` at introduction"] # [doc = " (the kernel's Value-level ops construct dense-class results — a `Sparse` claim would"] # [doc = " diverge from the runtime value, so it is refused, not silently re-classed). `vsa_permute`'s"] # [doc = " shift is any unsigned `Binary{W}` magnitude (rotation is cyclic — the inverse permute is"] # [doc = " the complementary shift `dim − s`); a bare-decimal shift has no width anchor here and must"] # [doc = " be written/ascribed explicitly (RFC-0012 §4.3 — never a default width)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `vsa_bundle` adds two static rules of its own: the model must additionally be in the"] # [doc = " **certified-bundle set {MAP-I}** — the only introduction-set model with a *certified*"] # [doc = " Value-level bundle (`bundle_values_certified`, the M-131 checked-instantiation pattern);"] # [doc = " an FHRR/BSC bundle is a **static** refusal naming the certified set (their kernel bundles"] # [doc = " are `Empirical`-profile ops — routing them here would silently re-tag their evidence,"] # [doc = " VR-5; surfacing them is a distinct append-only extension) — and the item count `N` lives"] # [doc = " in the `Seq` type, so an **empty bundle (`N = 0`) is a static refusal** too (no"] # [doc = " superposition is defined). ("gated by the matches! above") , } } # [doc = " Try to check a call to a **scalar-float prim** (ADR-040 §2.4/§2.5; M-898/M-899, `enb`"] # [doc = " Gap A) — the arithmetic group `flt_add`/`flt_sub`/`flt_mul`/`flt_div` (binary) and"] # [doc = " `flt_neg` (unary), kernel `flt.add`/`flt.sub`/`flt.mul`/`flt.div`/`flt.neg`, plus the"] # [doc = " comparison group `flt_lt`/`flt_le`/`flt_gt`/`flt_ge`/`flt_eq` and the named total order"] # [doc = " `flt_total_le` (all binary), kernel `flt.lt`/…/`flt.eq`/`flt.total_le` (surface names are"] # [doc = " `_`-joined like `dense_add`, since `.` is the path separator in the lexer). Returns"] # [doc = " `Ok(None)` if `name` is not one of them."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Float` is nullary (binary64 only at introduction — ADR-040 FLAG-1), so the static rule is"] # [doc = " the simplest of the prim branches: every operand must be exactly `Float`, and the result"] # [doc = " is `Float` for the arithmetic ops and **`Binary{1}`** — the realized `Bool` of the"] # [doc = " RFC-0032 D1 engineering note, exactly the `eq`/`lt` shape — for the comparison ops. A"] # [doc = " non-`Float` operand is an explicit refusal (a cross-paradigm edge needs an"] # [doc = " explicit `swap`; a bare decimal has no `Float` anchor — RFC-0012 §4.3 gives it none — so it"] # [doc = " is refused, never defaulted; write a float literal `1.0`, M-897). The numeric-domain"] # [doc = " behaviour is deliberately **not** a static rule and **not** a runtime refusal either:"] # [doc = " arithmetic specials (overflow → ±inf, `x/0` → ±inf, `0/0` → NaN) are **in-band,"] # [doc = " inspectable, propagating values** per the ratified ADR-040 §2.4 FLAG-2 — the ops are total"] # [doc = " over `Float` (contrast `div_u`, whose integer div-by-zero has no in-band sentinel and"] # [doc = " must refuse at runtime) — and **comparison NaN semantics are value semantics, not types**:"] # [doc = " the five predicates deliver the IEEE-754 §5.11 *defined* result `false` on any NaN operand"] # [doc = " (NaN is unordered; `flt_eq(NaN, NaN)` is false), while `flt_total_le` is the IEEE-754"] # [doc = " §5.10 `totalOrder` — total and reflexive, NaN placed last, `−0` before `+0` (ADR-040"] # [doc = " §2.4; M-899). Per-op tag: `Empirical` per ADR-040 §2.6, carried on the runtime value with"] # [doc = " its zero-deviation-vs-spec bound; for `flt_total_le` the total-order *property* stays"] # [doc = " `Empirical` until the M-511 proof debt is discharged (`mycelium-interp/src/prims.rs`)."] fn try_check_float_prim (& self , scope : & mut Vec < (String , Ty) > , head : & Expr , name : & str , args : & [Expr] ,) -> Result < Option < (Ty , Expr) > , CheckError > { if name == "bin_to_flt" { if args . len () != 1 { return self . err (format ! ("`bin_to_flt` takes 1 operand, got {} (ADR-040 §2.4; CU-3)" , args . len ())) ; } let (vty , v2) = self . check (scope , & args [0] , None) ? ; if ! matches ! (vty , Ty :: Binary (_)) { return self . err (format ! ("`bin_to_flt` operand must be a concrete `Binary{{N}}`, got {vty} (ADR-040 \

[v2])))) ; } if name == "flt_to_bin" { if args . len () != 2 { return self . err (format ! ("`flt_to_bin` takes 2 operands, got {} (ADR-040 §2.4; CU-3)" , args . len ())) ; } let (vty , v2) = self . check (scope , & args [0] , Some (& Ty :: Float)) ? ; if ! matches ! (vty , Ty :: Float) { return self . err (format ! ("`flt_to_bin` first (value) operand must be a `Float` (IEEE-754 binary64 — \

                     witness is refused)")) ; } ; return Ok (Some ((Ty :: Binary (Width :: Lit (m)) , app_node (head , vec ! [v2 , w2]) ,))) ; } let is_cmp = FLOAT_CMP_PRIM_NAMES . contains (& name) ; let is_class = FLOAT_CLASS_PRIM_NAMES . contains (& name) ; if ! is_cmp && ! is_class && ! FLOAT_ARITH_PRIM_NAMES . contains (& name) { return Ok (None) ; } let want = if name == "flt_neg" || is_class { 1 } else { 2 } ; if args . len () != want { return self . err (format ! ("`{name}` takes {want} operand(s), got {} (ADR-040 §2.4/§2.5; M-898/M-899)" , args . len ())) ; } let mut rebuilt = Vec :: with_capacity (want) ; for (i , a) in args . iter () . enumerate () { let (ty , a2) = self . check (scope , a , Some (& Ty :: Float)) ? ; if ! matches ! (ty , Ty :: Float) { let which = match (want , i) { (1 , _) => "single" , (_ , 0) => "first" , _ => "second" , } ; return self . err (format ! ("`{name}` {which} operand must be a `Float` (IEEE-754 binary64 — ADR-040 \

(List literals are checked by"] # [doc = " [`Self::check_list`], which has the element-expression context `lit_ty_of` lacks.)"] fn lit_ty (& self , l : & Literal) -> Result < Ty , CheckError > { lit_ty_of (self . site , l) } }

# [doc = " The literal-typing rule (Q6), as a free function so the elaborator can reuse it without a"] # [doc = " checking context. A literal *is* its representation (width = digit count); bare integers and"] # [doc = " lists are explicit refusals."] pub (crate) fn lit_ty_of (site : & str , l : & Literal) -> Result < Ty , CheckError > { match l { Literal :: Bin (s) => { let n = s . chars () . filter (| c | * c == '0' || * c == '1') . count () ; if n == 0 { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , "empty binary literal")) ; } Ok (Ty :: Binary (Width :: Lit (u32 :: try_from (n) . expect ("digit count fits u32") ,))) } Literal :: Trit (s) => { if s . is_empty () { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , "empty ternary literal")) ; } Ok (Ty :: Ternary (Width :: Lit (u32 :: try_from (s . len ()) . expect ("trit count fits u32") ,))) } Literal :: Int (_) => Err (CheckError :: new (site , "a bare integer literal has no representation family (no cross-family defaulting, \
             Q6) — write a binary/ternary literal, declare a `default paradigm` (RFC-0012), or \
             ascribe a Dense element" ,)) , Literal :: AmbientInt (_ , _) => Err (CheckError :: new (site , "internal: an unresolved ambient bare decimal reached `lit_ty_of` — the checker \
             resolves its width from context first (RFC-0012 §4.3)" ,)) , Literal :: Bytes (_) => Ok (Ty :: Bytes) , Literal :: Float (_) => Ok (Ty :: Float) , Literal :: Str (_) => Ok (Ty :: Bytes) , Literal :: List (_) => Err (CheckError :: new (site , "a list/sequence pattern is not supported in the v0 surface (RFC-0032 D3 — `[…]` is a \
             constructor literal, not a pattern)" ,)) , } }
gaps.reason: closure parameter `c` has no explicit type annotation — Mycelium's `lambda` parameters are always `name: Type` (grammar `lambda_expr` / `param`) and this transpiler has no type-inference pass to recover an omitted Rust closure-param type

# [doc = " Normalize a surface [`Pattern`] against its `expected` type into a [`crate::usefulness::Pat`]"] # [doc = " (the coverage-matrix shape), collecting its binders as `(name, type, occurrence)` — the"] # [doc = " **occurrence** is the path of field indices from the scrutinee root to the binder's position."] # [doc = " Shared by the checker (`Cx::check_pattern`, occurrence `[]`) and the **elaborator** (which needs"] # [doc = " the matrix + the binder occurrences to lower a `match` to flat L0 `Match`/binders — RFC-0011"] # [doc = " §4.4). Nested constructor/literal patterns recurse (RFC-0007 §4.7); a binder is a wildcard for"] # [doc = " coverage (it refines nothing), a nullary constructor an empty `Ctor`."] pub (crate) fn normalize_pattern (types : & BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > , site : & str , pat : & Pattern , expected : & Ty , occ : & [usize] , binds : & mut Vec < (String , Ty , Vec < usize >) > ,) -> Result < crate :: usefulness :: Pat , CheckError > { use crate :: usefulness :: Pat ; match pat { Pattern :: Wildcard => Ok (Pat :: Wild) , Pattern :: Ident (n) => { if let Ty :: Data (tn , _) = expected { let d = lookup_data_home_checked (types , tn , site) ? ; if let Some (c) = d . ctors . iter () . find (| c | c . name == * n) { if ! c . fields . is_empty () { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("constructor pattern `{n}` must bind its {} field(s) (W7)" , c . fields . len ()) ,)) ; } return Ok (Pat :: Ctor (n . clone () , vec ! [])) ; } } binds . push ((n . clone () , expected . clone () , occ . to_vec ())) ; Ok (Pat :: Wild) } Pattern :: Ctor (n , subs) => { let Ty :: Data (tn , targs) = expected else { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("constructor pattern `{n}` on a {expected} scrutinee — match a literal or `_`") ,)) ; } ; let d = lookup_data_home_checked (types , tn , site) ? . clone () ; let Some (c) = d . ctors . iter () . find (| c | c . name == * n) else { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("`{n}` is not a constructor of {tn}") ,)) ; } ; if subs . len () != c . fields . len () { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("pattern `{n}` binds {} of {} field(s) (W7: exactly the arity)" , subs . len () , c . fields . len ()) ,)) ; } let s = param_subst (& d . params , targs) ; let mut out = Vec :: with_capacity (subs . len ()) ; for (i , (sub , fty)) in subs . iter () . zip (& c . fields) . enumerate () { let mut child = occ . to_vec () ; child . push (i) ; let fty = subst_ty (fty , & s) ; out . push (normalize_pattern (types , site , sub , & fty , & child , binds) ?) ; } Ok (Pat :: Ctor (n . clone () , out)) } Pattern :: Tuple (subs) => { let n = subs . len () ; let ctor_name = tuple_ctor_name (n) ; normalize_pattern (types , site , & Pattern :: Ctor (ctor_name . clone () , subs . clone ()) , expected , occ , binds ,) . map_err (| e | { CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("tuple pattern `({})` — {}" , subs . iter () . map (| _ | "_") . collect ::< Vec < _ >> () . join (", ") , e . message) ,) }) } Pattern :: Lit (lit) => { if matches ! (lit , Literal :: Float (_)) { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , "a float literal is not a legal match pattern (ADR-040 FLAG-4: IEEE equality \
                     and content identity diverge on floats — `-0.0`/NaN; match on the result of \
                     an explicit comparison instead — never a silent equality choice, G2)" ,)) ; } let lty = lit_ty_of (site , lit) ? ; if lty != * expected { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("literal pattern has type {lty} but the scrutinee is {expected} \
                         (W7: a literal arm must match the scrutinee's repr and width)") ,)) ; } Ok (Pat :: Lit (literal_key (lit))) } Pattern :: Or (_) => Err (CheckError :: new (site , "internal: Pattern::Or reached normalize_pattern — or-patterns must be desugared \
             in check_match before any downstream pass (invariant violation — report this)" ,)) , } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Re-infer an expression's type against a checked [`Env`] (the elaborator needs the scrutinee type"] # [doc = " to lower a `match`, and a `let`-bound's type to track its scope — RFC-0011). The program is"] # [doc = " already checked, so this recomputes a type the checker validated; it does not re-litigate errors."] pub (crate) fn infer_type (env : & Env , scope : & mut Vec < (String , Ty) > , e : & Expr ,) -> Result < Ty , CheckError > { let no_imports = NoduleImports :: default () ; let cx = Cx { site : "<elaborate>" , types : & env . types , fns : & env . fns , traits : & env . traits , instances : & env . instances , imports : & no_imports , lower_rules : & env . lower_rules , tyvars : & [] , bounds : & [] , std_sys : true , depth : Cell :: new (0) , affine : Tracker :: inert () , # [cfg (test)] stage3_sabotage_skip_substitution : false , } ; cx . infer (scope , e) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **Test-only** twin of [`infer_type`] with an **active** affine tracker"] # [doc = " ([`Tracker::seeded`], seeded from `scope` — the caller's own pre-existing local bindings, e.g."] # [doc = " a `Substrate`-typed local a fixture wants tracked), instead of `infer_type`'s deliberate"] # [doc = " [`Tracker::inert`]. Needed because every M-1054 Stage 1b/Stage 3 white-box fixture checks a bare"] # [doc = " call `Expr` directly (no surface grammar for `value_params` yet — DN-110 §8.6, so there is no"] # [doc = " `check_nodule`/`fn`-body route to a real, active-tracker `Cx`) via `infer_type`, whose `inert`"] # [doc = " tracker is exactly right for its own (post-check re-inference) job but makes every `self.affine`"] # [doc = " interaction inside [`Cx::check_sugar_call`]'s Stage-3 (OQ-H4/DN-117) mechanism a no-op — the"] # [doc = " per-argument tracker-touch trigger and the substituted-`Expr` double-consume walk both need a"] # [doc = " real, active tracker to do anything. `#[cfg(test)]`-gated (never a shipped alternate entry"] # [doc = " point — the one production `infer_type` stays `Tracker::inert()`, unconditionally)."] # [cfg (test)] pub (crate) fn infer_type_with_active_affine (env : & Env , scope : & mut Vec < (String , Ty) > , e : & Expr ,) -> Result < Ty , CheckError > { infer_type_with_active_affine_inner (env , scope , e , false) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A canonical key for de-duplicating literal patterns (M-320): normalize away `_` separators so"] # [doc = " `0b1010` and `0b10_10` collide as the *same* literal. `Bin`/`Trit`/`Bytes`/`Str` reach here (the"] # [doc = " caller type-checks the literal first, which rejects `Int`/`List`)."] fn literal_key (lit : & Literal) -> String { match lit { Literal :: Bin (s) => format ! ("b:{}" , s . chars () . filter (| c | * c == '0' || * c == '1') . collect ::< String > ()) , Literal :: Trit (s) => format ! ("t:{s}") , Literal :: Int (i) => format ! ("i:{i}") , Literal :: AmbientInt (p , i) => format ! ("amb:{p}:{i}") , Literal :: Bytes (s) => { format ! ("by:{}" , s . chars () . filter (| c | * c != '_') . collect ::< String > ()) } Literal :: Str (s) => format ! ("s:{s}") , Literal :: Float (s) => format ! ("f:{s}") , Literal :: List (_) => "list" . to_owned () , } }
gaps.reason: unsupported expression form `format ! ("b:{}" , s . chars () . filter (| c | * c == '0' || * c == '1') . collect ::< String > ())`

# [doc = " The teaching diagnostic for imperative control-flow words used as names (RFC-0007 §4.8):"] # [doc = " the error was happening anyway (unknown name) — make it teach instead of confuse."] fn teach_unknown (name : & str , base : & str) -> String { if matches ! (name , "while" | "loop" | "break" | "continue" | "return") { format ! ("{base} — `{name}` is not a Mycelium form; iterate by recursion or \
             `for x in xs, acc = init => body` (RFC-0007 §4.8)") } else { base . to_owned () } }

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " The v0 linear-recursion shape check (RFC-0007 §4.8): every constructor of `tname` is either"] # [doc = " a **nil** (no fields) or a **cons** (exactly one spine field of type `tname` + exactly one"] # [doc = " element field), with one element type across all cons constructors. Returns the element"] # [doc = " type; anything else is an explicit refusal."] fn linear_elem_ty (site : & str , types : & BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > , tname : & str , targs : & [Ty] ,) -> Result < Ty , CheckError > { let d = lookup_data (types , tname) . ok_or_else (| | CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("unknown type `{tname}`"))) ? ; let s = param_subst (& d . params , targs) ; let mut elem : Option < Ty > = None ; let mut has_cons = false ; for c in & d . ctors { if c . fields . is_empty () { continue ; } let (spine , rest) : (Vec < & Ty > , Vec < & Ty >) = c . fields . iter () . partition (| f | matches ! (f , Ty :: Data (n , _) if n == tname)) ; if spine . len () != 1 || rest . len () != 1 { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("`for` needs a linearly recursive type: constructor `{}` of `{tname}` must \
                     have exactly one `{tname}` field and one element field (general \
                     catamorphisms are an L2 concern — RFC-0007 §4.8)" , c . name) ,)) ; } has_cons = true ; let elem_ty = subst_ty (rest [0] , & s) ; match & elem { None => elem = Some (elem_ty) , Some (e) if * e == elem_ty => { } Some (e) => { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("`for` needs one element type across `{tname}`'s constructors: \
                         {e} vs {elem_ty}") ,)) } } } if ! has_cons { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("`{tname}` has no recursive constructor — nothing for `for` to iterate") ,)) ; } Ok (elem . expect ("has_cons implies an element type")) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The paradigm name of a representation type (for the never-silent cross-paradigm framing)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `pub(crate)` (widened from private, zero logic change) so the M-1013 Stage-5 self-hosting"] # [doc = " differential (`compiler_stage5_classify.rs`) can call this real oracle directly, exactly as the"] # [doc = " eval PR-1 leaf widened `try_match` (DN-26 §10.2 harness marshalling)."] pub (crate) fn paradigm_name (t : & Ty) -> Option < & 'static str > { match t { Ty :: Binary (_) => Some ("Binary") , Ty :: Ternary (_) => Some ("Ternary") , Ty :: Dense (_ , _) => Some ("Dense") , Ty :: Vsa { .. } => Some ("VSA") , Ty :: Seq (_ , _) | Ty :: Bytes | Ty :: Float | Ty :: Data (_ , _) | Ty :: Substrate (_) | Ty :: Var (_) | Ty :: Fn (_ , _) => None , } }

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " Frame a type-edge mismatch (RFC-0012 §4.4): a **cross-paradigm** edge (`want` and `got` are"] # [doc = " representation types of *different* paradigms) becomes an explicit `MissingConversion` pointing"] # [doc = " at writing a `swap` — the never-silent guarantee. A same-paradigm mismatch (e.g. two `Binary`"] # [doc = " widths) keeps the plain wording."] fn edge_mismatch (edge : & str , want : & Ty , got : & Ty) -> String { match (paradigm_name (want) , paradigm_name (got)) { (Some (w) , Some (g)) if w != g => format ! ("MissingConversion: {edge} is {got} but {want} is required — a cross-paradigm edge needs \
             an explicit `swap(…, to: {want}, policy: …)` (RFC-0012 §4.4; never silently converted)") , _ => format ! ("{edge} has type {got}, expected {want}") , } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Encode a non-negative decimal `v` as an **unsigned** `Binary{width}` literal (MSB-first), or an"] # [doc = " explicit refusal if it does not fit (RFC-0012 §4.3 — never a silent wrap/truncation)."] fn encode_binary (site : & str , v : i64 , width : u32) -> Result < Literal , CheckError > { if width == 0 { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , "cannot encode a decimal at Binary{0} (zero width)" ,)) ; } let vu = u128 :: try_from (v) . map_err (| _ | { CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("negative decimal `{v}` has no unsigned `Binary` encoding") ,) }) ? ; if width < 128 && (vu >> width) != 0 { return Err (CheckError :: new (site , format ! ("decimal `{v}` does not fit Binary{{{width}}} (unsigned range 0..2^{width})") ,)) ; } let mut s = String :: with_capacity (width as usize) ; for i in (0 .. width) . rev () { let bit = if i < 128 { (vu >> i) & 1 } else { 0 } ; s . push (if bit == 1 { '1' } else { '0' }) ; } Ok (Literal :: Bin (s)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Canonicalize a surface VSA model ident to the **kernel model id** (`Repr::Vsa { model }` —"] # [doc = " M-892).
