gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The **recursive** strongly-connected components of the reachable call graph, **callee-first**"] # [doc = " (a callee SCC is bound *outside* its callers). A self-recursive singleton (`{f}` with a self-call)"] # [doc = " and a mutual group (≥2 functions in a cycle) are both recursive SCCs; a function in no cycle"] # [doc = " inlines and is **not** returned. Computed with Tarjan's algorithm — which finalises each SCC only"] # [doc = " after all its successor (callee) SCCs, i.e. in reverse-topological = callee-first order. Roots,"] # [doc = " successors, and each SCC's members are visited/sorted deterministically so the lowering (and thus"] # [doc = " the content hash) is reproducible. A function is \"reachable\" if the entry transitively calls it."] fn recursive_sccs (env : & Env , entry : & str) -> Result < Vec < Vec < String > > , ElabError > { let mut reachable : BTreeSet < String > = BTreeSet :: new () ; let mut frontier = vec ! [entry . to_owned ()] ; while let Some (f) = frontier . pop () { if ! reachable . insert (f . clone ()) { continue ; } if let Some (fd) = env . fns . get (& f) { for callee in calls_in_fn (& f , & fd . body) ? { if env . fns . contains_key (& callee) { frontier . push (callee) ; } } } } struct Tarjan < 'e > { env : & 'e Env , reachable : & 'e BTreeSet < String > , index : usize , idx : BTreeMap < String , usize > , low : BTreeMap < String , usize > , on_stack : BTreeSet < String > , stack : Vec < String > , out : Vec < Vec < String > > , } fn successors (env : & Env , reachable : & BTreeSet < String > , f : & str ,) -> Result < BTreeSet < String > , ElabError > { Ok (calls_in_fn (f , & env . fns [f] . body) ? . into_iter () . filter (| c | reachable . contains (c) && env . fns . contains_key (c)) . collect ()) } fn strongconnect (t : & mut Tarjan , v : & str) -> Result < () , ElabError > { t . idx . insert (v . to_owned () , t . index) ; t . low . insert (v . to_owned () , t . index) ; t . index += 1 ; t . stack . push (v . to_owned ()) ; t . on_stack . insert (v . to_owned ()) ; for w in successors (t . env , t . reachable , v) ? { if ! t . idx . contains_key (& w) { strongconnect (t , & w) ? ; let lw = t . low [& w] ; let lv = t . low . get_mut (v) . expect ("v indexed") ; * lv = (* lv) . min (lw) ; } else if t . on_stack . contains (& w) { let iw = t . idx [& w] ; let lv = t . low . get_mut (v) . expect ("v indexed") ; * lv = (* lv) . min (iw) ; } } if t . low [v] == t . idx [v] { let mut scc : Vec < String > = Vec :: new () ; loop { let w = t . stack . pop () . expect ("stack non-empty while popping an SCC") ; t . on_stack . remove (& w) ; let is_root = w == v ; scc . push (w) ; if is_root { break ; } } scc . sort () ; t . out . push (scc) ; } Ok (()) } let mut t = Tarjan { env , reachable : & reachable , index : 0 , idx : BTreeMap :: new () , low : BTreeMap :: new () , on_stack : BTreeSet :: new () , stack : Vec :: new () , out : Vec :: new () , } ; for f in & reachable { if ! t . idx . contains_key (f) { strongconnect (& mut t , f) ? ; } } let mut sccs = Vec :: with_capacity (t . out . len ()) ; for scc in t . out { let recursive = scc . len () > 1 || calls_in_fn (& scc [0] , & env . fns [& scc [0]] . body) ? . contains (& scc [0]) ; if recursive { sccs . push (scc) ; } } Ok (sccs) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Build the content-addressed data registry `Σ` (RFC-0001 §4.3 r3) from the checked environment's"] # [doc = " type declarations, so the elaborator can resolve constructor names to `#T#i` [`CtorRef`]s. A type"] # [doc = " carrying a field outside the r3 data fragment (e.g. a `Substrate` field) is skipped; if a"] # [doc = " *reachable* type references it, the registry build fails and the program is honestly `Residual`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Public so a differential / a consumer can rebuild the **same** registry the elaborator used"] # [doc = " (it is a pure, content-addressed function of `env.types`) — e.g. to map an L1 evaluator's"] # [doc = " name-keyed data value onto the elaborated L0 value's `#T#i` identity (NFR-7)."] pub fn build_registry (env : & Env) -> Result < DataRegistry , ElabError > { let mut specs : BTreeMap < String , DeclSpec > = BTreeMap :: new () ; 'types : for (name , d) in & env . types { let mut ctors = Vec :: with_capacity (d . ctors . len ()) ; for c in & d . ctors { let mut fields = Vec :: with_capacity (c . fields . len ()) ; for f in & c . fields { match field_spec (& env . types , f) { Some (fs) => fields . push (fs) , None => continue 'types , } } ctors . push (CtorSpec { fields }) ; } specs . insert (name . clone () , DeclSpec { ctors }) ; } DataRegistry :: build (& specs) . map_err (| e | ElabError :: Residual { site : "<data registry>" . to_owned () , what : format ! ("a reachable data type is outside the r3 fragment: {e}") , }) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The bounded EXPLAIN record of tail-call optimization (RFC-0041 §4.6 tco32): total elided frames,"] # [doc = " a per-callee iteration tally, and a **ring buffer of the last [`TcoTrace::RING_CAP`] elisions** —"] # [doc = " so an over-budget error at the end of a deep tail chain is diagnosable (which callee, how deep)"] # [doc = " without retaining an unbounded trace. Inspectable via [`Evaluator::tco_trace`] (never a black"] # [doc = " box — house rule #2)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Default , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct TcoTrace { # [doc = " The total number of tail-call frames elided across every [`Evaluator::call`] so far."] pub total_elided : u64 , # [doc = " Per-callee elision counts (identity + iteration count — §4.6 tco32)."] pub per_callee : BTreeMap < String , u64 > , # [doc = " The last [`Self::RING_CAP`] elisions, newest at the back (the bounded ring buffer)."] pub recent : VecDeque < TcoElision > , }

gaps.snippet: impl TcoTrace { # [doc = " The ring-buffer capacity: the number of most-recent elisions retained for the actionable"] # [doc = " trace. Bounded so a deep tail chain cannot grow the trace without limit (§4.6 tco32)."] pub const RING_CAP : usize = 32 ; # [doc = " Record one elided tail frame for `callee`: bump the totals and push onto the bounded ring."] fn record (& mut self , callee : & str) { self . total_elided = self . total_elided . saturating_add (1) ; let iteration = { let c = self . per_callee . entry (callee . to_owned ()) . or_insert (0) ; * c = c . saturating_add (1) ; * c } ; if self . recent . len () == Self :: RING_CAP { self . recent . pop_front () ; } self . recent . push_back (TcoElision { callee : callee . to_owned () , iteration , }) ; } }

impl < 'e > Evaluator < 'e > { # [doc = " An evaluator over `env` with the trusted default engines and the default budgets."] # [must_use] pub fn new (env : & 'e Env) -> Self { Evaluator { env , prims : PrimRegistry :: with_builtins () , swap : Box :: new (BinaryTernarySwapEngine) , fuel : DEFAULT_FUEL , depth : DEFAULT_DEPTH , releases : std :: sync :: Mutex :: new (Vec :: new ()) , forage_trail : std :: sync :: Mutex :: new (Vec :: new ()) , tco_trace : std :: sync :: Mutex :: new (TcoTrace :: default ()) , } } # [doc = " The [`ForageDecision`] EXPLAIN trail accumulated by every `@forage(policy)` placement"] # [doc = " decision (M-906; DN-70 D1; RFC-0008 RT3) across every [`Self::call`] made on this"] # [doc = " `Evaluator` so far — mandatory EXPLAIN, never a black box (house rule 2; RFC-0005 §2.2)."] # [doc = " Empty iff no `@forage`-annotated hypha ever ran. A poisoned lock (only reachable after an"] # [doc = " unrelated panic while holding it, which this evaluator never does by design) is recovered"] # [doc = " rather than propagated (mirrors [`Self::release_events`])."] # [must_use] pub fn forage_decisions (& self) -> Vec < ForageDecision > { self . forage_trail . lock () . unwrap_or_else (std :: sync :: PoisonError :: into_inner) . clone () } # [doc = " The [`TcoTrace`] EXPLAIN record accumulated by tail-call optimization (RFC-0041 §4.6 tco32)"] # [doc = " across every [`Self::call`] made on this `Evaluator` so far — the bounded per-callee tally +"] # [doc = " ring buffer of the most-recent elided tail frames, so a deep tail chain that ends in an"] # [doc = " over-budget error yields an actionable trace (house rule #2 — never a black box). Empty iff"] # [doc = " no tail call was ever elided. A poisoned lock (only reachable after an unrelated panic while"] # [doc = " holding it, which this evaluator never does by design) is recovered rather than propagated"] # [doc = " (mirrors [`Self::release_events`])."] # [must_use] pub fn tco_trace (& self) -> TcoTrace { self . tco_trace . lock () . unwrap_or_else (std :: sync :: PoisonError :: into_inner) . clone () } # [doc = " The [`ReleaseEvent`](crate::substrate::ReleaseEvent) log accumulated by scope-exit releases"] # [doc = " (M-904; DN-71 §8 FLAG-4's v0 drop-without-consume posture) across every [`Self::call`] made"] # [doc = " on this `Evaluator` so far — inspectable, never a black box (house rule 2). Empty iff no live"] # [doc = " `Substrate` binding was ever abandoned (every one reached either escaped into its scope's own"] # [doc = " result, or had already been explicitly `consume`d). A poisoned lock (only reachable if a prior"] # [doc = " panic occurred while holding it, which this evaluator never does by design) is recovered"] # [doc = " rather than propagated, so an unrelated panic elsewhere can never make this accessor itself"] # [doc = " panic or silently report an empty log (G2)."] # [must_use] pub fn release_events (& self) -> Vec < crate :: substrate :: ReleaseEvent > { self . releases . lock () . unwrap_or_else (std :: sync :: PoisonError :: into_inner) . clone () } # [doc = " M-904 (DN-71 §8 FLAG-4 v0 posture): release `popped` at its scope-exit iff it is a still-live"] # [doc = " `Substrate` handle that does **not** escape into `escaping` (the enclosing scope's own"] # [doc = " result — checked via [`value_contains_substrate_id`], not assumed, so a returned handle, or"] # [doc = " one nested inside a constructed `Data` value, is never wrongly released). Records a"] # [doc = " [`ReleaseEvent`](crate::substrate::ReleaseEvent) into [`Self::releases`] when a release"] # [doc = " actually happens. A non-`Substrate` binding, an already-terminal handle (already `consume`d,"] # [doc = " or already released through another clone of the same identity), or an escaping handle are"] # [doc = " all legitimate no-ops here — nothing to release, never an error."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Known v0 limitation (honest, not silently hidden — mirrors `crate::affine`'s documented"] # [doc = " loop/closure gap):** this is called at the two scope-exit points M-904 wires it into —"] # [doc = " `Expr::Let` and a function's own parameters at the end of [`Self::invoke`] — not at every"] # [doc = " binder in the evaluator (e.g. a `match`-arm pattern binder that captures a `Substrate` out of"] # [doc = " a data field is not yet covered). A handle abandoned only through such an uncovered binder is"] # [doc = " not released here; closing that gap is future work, not silently claimed done."] fn release_if_abandoned (& self , popped : & (String , L1Value) , escaping : Option < & L1Value >) { let L1Value :: Substrate (handle) = & popped . 1 else { return ; } ; if let Some (v) = escaping { if value_contains_substrate_id (v , handle . id ()) { return ; } } if let Some (event) = handle . release (popped . 0 . clone ()) { self . releases . lock () . unwrap_or_else (std :: sync :: PoisonError :: into_inner) . push (event) ; } } # [doc = " Replace the prim registry and swap engine. The swap engine must be `Send + Sync` (all"] # [doc = " built-in engines are `Copy`, hence `Send + Sync`; a custom engine for tests likewise)."] # [must_use] pub fn with_engines (mut self , prims : PrimRegistry , swap : Box < dyn SwapEngine + Send + Sync > ,) -> Self { self . prims = prims ; self . swap = swap ; self } # [doc = " Override the step budget."] # [must_use] pub fn with_fuel (mut self , fuel : u64) -> Self { self . fuel = fuel ; self } # [doc = " Override the recursion-depth budget. Evaluation runs on the deep worker stack"] # [doc = " ([`mycelium_stack`]), so a raised budget is host-stack-safe — the budget is the ceiling,"] # [doc = " not the host stack."] # [must_use] pub fn with_depth (mut self , depth : u32) -> Self { self . depth = depth ; self } # [doc = " Apply a budget [`EvaluatorOpts`] in one call — equivalent to"] # [doc = " `self.with_fuel(opts.fuel).with_depth(opts.depth)`. Additive convenience; the engines are"] # [doc = " untouched (configure those with [`Evaluator::with_engines`])."] # [must_use] pub fn with_opts (self , opts : EvaluatorOpts) -> Self { self . with_fuel (opts . fuel) . with_depth (opts . depth) } # [doc = " Call function `name` with `args`, big-step, under the configured budgets. let mut fuel = self . fuel ; let mut ledger = Budgets :: new () ; self . run_machine (& budget , & mut fuel , & mut ledger , name , args) }) } # [doc = " Map a surface effect name to its [`EffectKind`] and create the corresponding"] # [doc = " [`EffectBudget`] variant with the given ceiling (RFC-0014 §4.5 I3/I4; M-677)."] # [doc = " The mapping is closed: the five built-in kinds plus a fall-through `Named` bucket for any"] # [doc = " user-declared name. `\"retry\"` → `Attempts`, `\"alloc\"` → `Bytes`, `\"io\"` → `Ops`,"] # [doc = " `\"cascade\"` → `Depth`, `\"time\"` → `Fuel`, any other → `Named`."] fn effect_name_to_budget (name : & str , ceiling : u64) -> EffectBudget { match name { "retry" => EffectBudget :: Attempts (ceiling) , "alloc" => EffectBudget :: Bytes (ceiling) , "io" => EffectBudget :: Ops (ceiling) , "cascade" => EffectBudget :: Depth (ceiling) , "time" => EffectBudget :: Fuel (ceiling) , other => EffectBudget :: Named (other . to_owned () , ceiling) , } } # [doc = " **The L1 work-stack CEK machine (RFC-0041 §4.1/§4.6, M-979).** The former 7-function"] # [doc = " recursive SCC (`invoke`/`eval`/`eval_app`/`eval_match`/`eval_for`/`eval_wild`/"] # [doc = " `eval_hypha_forage`) is now **one iterative loop** over an explicit heap work-stack"] # [doc = " (`Vec<Frame>`): each [`Frame`] reifies the interleaved *post-child* work (scope push/pop,"] # [doc = " the M-904 `release_if_abandoned`, the return-guarantee assert, the swap/ascription checks) as"] # [doc = " an explicit continuation, so control recursion is **O(1) host stack** for any input depth —"] # [doc = " the depth budget, not the host stack, is what refuses a pathological input (never-silent, G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The environment is a single shared `scope: Vec<(name, value)>` with a per-function `base`"] # [doc = " marker (each function sees only `scope[base..]`, preserving lexical scope across calls). gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A reified **instance selection** (RFC-0019 §4.4; house rule #2 — no black boxes). When mono"] # [doc = " lowers a trait-method call to a direct call, it records *which* instance it picked: the trait, the"] # [doc = " concrete receiver type, and the mangled name of the emitted method function. The dispatch choice"] # [doc = " is thus programmatically inspectable (`EXPLAIN`-able), not hidden inside the rewrite."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct InstanceSelection { # [doc = " The trait whose method was called."] pub trait_name : String , # [doc = " The concrete receiver type the instance is `for` (the full type, not the head — e.g."] # [doc = " `Binary{8}`, never just `Binary`)."] pub for_ty : Ty , # [doc = " The mangled name of the monomorphic function mono emitted for this instance's method (the"] # [doc = " direct callee the trait-method call was rewritten to — e.g. `cmp$Cmp$Binary8`)."] pub impl_mangled : String , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Like [`monomorphize`] but also returns the [`MonoSelections`] EXPLAIN record of every trait-method"] # [doc = " dispatch resolved (house rule #2 — the static resolution is inspectable, not a black box)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " See [`monomorphize`]."] pub fn monomorphize_with_selections (env : & Env , entry : & str ,) -> Result < (Env , MonoSelections) , ElabError > { if is_already_monomorphic (env) { return Ok ((env . clone () , MonoSelections :: default ())) ; } let mut m = Mono :: new (env) ; m . run (entry) ? ; m . finish () }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Is `env` already fully monomorphic, trait-free, **and** HOF-free? Then mono is the identity"] # [doc = " (the fast pass-through). True iff **no** function is generic, **no** function has a fn-typed"] # [doc = " value parameter (which needs defunctionalization — RFC-0024 §4, M-687), **no** data type is"] # [doc = " generic, and there are **no** (user) traits / instances / retained impls."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **M-965 note:** every `Env` now carries the built-in `Fuse` prelude trait"] # [doc = " ([`crate::fuse::TRAIT_NAME`]) even when the program never mentions `fuse`/`Fuse` — mirroring how"] # [doc = " every `Env` already carries the built-in `Bool` prelude *type* (which doesn't break this check,"] # [doc = " since `Bool` happens to have empty `params`). A trait registration with **zero** instances/impls"] # [doc = " contributes nothing to specialize (an unimplemented trait can't be called — `check_fuse`/generic"] # [doc = " dispatch always requires a resolved instance), so the trait-emptiness test here is *specifically*"] # [doc = " \"no **user-declared** trait\" — the always-present builtin is excluded, exactly as `Bool` is"] # [doc = " excluded from the phylum-wide coherence view (`checkty::check_phylum_inner`'s `name != \"Bool\"`"] # [doc = " guard). `env.instances`/`env.impls` being empty is unaffected and still does the real work: a"] # [doc = " program that actually declares `impl Fuse[T] for T` has a non-empty `instances`/`impls`, which"] # [doc = " correctly still forces the full (specializing) pass."] fn is_already_monomorphic (env : & Env) -> bool { env . fns . values () . all (| fd | { fd . sig . params . is_empty () && fd . sig . value_params . iter () . all (| p | ! param_has_fn_type (& env . types , & fd . sig . param_names () , & p . ty)) && ! body_has_lambda (& fd . body) && ! body_has_fn_value (env , & fd . body) }) && env . types . values () . all (| d | d . params . is_empty ()) && env . traits . keys () . all (| name | name == crate :: fuse :: TRAIT_NAME || name == crate :: ord3 :: TRAIT_NAME) && env . instances . is_empty () && env . impls . is_empty () }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " RFC-0024 §4A.4 (M-704): true iff `e` references a top-level **function name as a value** — a"] # [doc = " single-segment `Expr::Path` naming a fn, occurring **not** as an application head (an applied fn"] # [doc = " `f(x)` is an ordinary first-order call, not a fn value). Such a reference needs the closure"] # [doc = " lowering (a named-fn-as-escaping-value becomes a nullary closure constructor), so the program is"] # [doc = " **not** mono's fast-path identity. Over-detection is safe — it only ever widens to the full pass."] fn body_has_fn_value (env : & Env , e : & Expr) -> bool { match e { Expr :: Path (p) => p . 0 . len () == 1 && env . fns . contains_key (& p . 0 [0]) , Expr :: Lit (Literal :: List (elems)) => elems . iter () . any (| x | body_has_fn_value (env , x)) , Expr :: Lit (_) => false , Expr :: Let { bound , body , .. } => { body_has_fn_value (env , bound) || body_has_fn_value (env , body) } Expr :: If { cond , conseq , alt } => { body_has_fn_value (env , cond) || body_has_fn_value (env , conseq) || body_has_fn_value (env , alt) } Expr :: Match { scrutinee , arms } => { body_has_fn_value (env , scrutinee) || arms . iter () . any (| a | body_has_fn_value (env , & a . body)) } Expr :: For { xs , init , body , .. } => { body_has_fn_value (env , xs) || body_has_fn_value (env , init) || body_has_fn_value (env , body) } Expr :: Swap { value , .. } => body_has_fn_value (env , value) , Expr :: WithParadigm { body , .. } => body_has_fn_value (env , body) , Expr :: Wild (b) | Expr :: Spore (b) | Expr :: Consume (b) | Expr :: Wrapping (b) | Expr :: Try (b) => { body_has_fn_value (env , b) } Expr :: Colony (hyphae) => hyphae . iter () . any (| h | body_has_fn_value (env , & h . body)) , Expr :: Lambda { body , .. } => body_has_fn_value (env , body) , Expr :: App { head : _ , args } => args . iter () . any (| x | body_has_fn_value (env , x)) , Expr :: Fuse { left , right } => body_has_fn_value (env , left) || body_has_fn_value (env , right) , Expr :: Reclaim { policy , body } => { body_has_fn_value (env , policy) || body_has_fn_value (env , body) } Expr :: Ascribe (inner , _) => body_has_fn_value (env , inner) , Expr :: TupleLit (elems) => elems . iter () . any (| x | body_has_fn_value (env , x)) , } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " True iff `e` contains an `Expr::Lambda` anywhere (RFC-0024 §4A, M-704) — the gate that keeps a"] # [doc = " closure-bearing program out of mono's fast pass-through (it needs the §4A lowering)."] fn body_has_lambda (e : & Expr) -> bool { match e { Expr :: Lambda { .. } => true , Expr :: Lit (Literal :: List (elems)) => elems . iter () . any (body_has_lambda) , Expr :: Lit (_) | Expr :: Path (_) => false , Expr :: Let { bound , body , .. } => body_has_lambda (bound) || body_has_lambda (body) , Expr :: If { cond , conseq , alt } => { body_has_lambda (cond) || body_has_lambda (conseq) || body_has_lambda (alt) } Expr :: Match { scrutinee , arms } => { body_has_lambda (scrutinee) || arms . iter () . any (| a | body_has_lambda (& a . body)) } Expr :: For { xs , init , body , .. } => { body_has_lambda (xs) || body_has_lambda (init) || body_has_lambda (body) } Expr :: Swap { value , .. } => body_has_lambda (value) , Expr :: WithParadigm { body , .. } => body_has_lambda (body) , Expr :: Wild (b) | Expr :: Spore (b) | Expr :: Consume (b) | Expr :: Wrapping (b) | Expr :: Try (b) => { body_has_lambda (b) } Expr :: Colony (hyphae) => hyphae . iter () . any (| h | body_has_lambda (& h . body)) , Expr :: App { head , args } => body_has_lambda (head) || args . iter () . any (body_has_lambda) , Expr :: Fuse { left , right } => body_has_lambda (left) || body_has_lambda (right) , Expr :: Reclaim { policy , body } => body_has_lambda (policy) || body_has_lambda (body) , Expr :: Ascribe (inner , _) => body_has_lambda (inner) , Expr :: TupleLit (elems) => elems . iter () . any (body_has_lambda) , } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " True iff the parameter type `t` resolves to (or contains) a `Ty::Fn` — meaning this parameter"] # [doc = " is a HOF that needs defunctionalization (RFC-0024 §4, M-687). Best-effort: a resolution failure"] # [doc = " is treated as \"not fn-typed\" (the full mono pass will catch it with an explicit Residual)."] fn param_has_fn_type (types : & BTreeMap < String , crate :: checkty :: DataInfo > , tyvars : & [String] , t : & TypeRef ,) -> bool { use crate :: ast :: BaseType ; match & t . base { BaseType :: Fn (_ , _) => true , BaseType :: Named (n , args) => { if tyvars . contains (n) || types . contains_key (n . as_str ()) { return false ; } args . iter () . any (| a | param_has_fn_type (types , tyvars , a)) } _ => false , } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A monomorphization work item — the unit of the dedup worklist. Deduplication is by the item's"] # [doc = " canonical [`item_key`] (a discriminant-tagged mangled string), so a `BTreeSet<String>` of seen"] # [doc = " keys guarantees each specialization is emitted **once** (dedup ⟹ the recursive walk terminates)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub (crate) enum Item { # [doc = " A function instance: the source fn `name` at concrete type arguments `targs` (empty for a"] # [doc = " monomorphic fn — which mangles to `name` unchanged), optionally specialised by resolved"] # [doc = " **function-argument** identities (RFC-0024 §4, M-687). `fn_args` carries `(param_index,"] # [doc = " callee_mangled_name)` for each value-parameter whose type is `Ty::Fn`; empty means no"] # [doc = " higher-order specialization. Such a parameter stays in the emitted"] # [doc = " signature typed `Fn$<arrow>`; an application of it inside the body lowers to"] # [doc = " `apply$<arrow>(f, x)`. Sorted by index (deterministic), baked into the item key so a HOF"] # [doc = " specialized dynamically is distinct from its static (or un-)specialization (G2)."] dyn_fns : Vec < (usize , String) > , } , # [doc = " A data-type instance: the source type `name` at concrete `targs`."] Data { name : String , targs : Vec < Ty > } , # [doc = " A trait-method instance: the unqualified method `method` of trait `trait_name`, resolved at the"] # [doc = " concrete receiver `for_ty` (the coherent instance's method, emitted as a direct fn)."] Method { trait_name : String , method : String , for_ty : Ty , } , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " RFC-0024 §4A.3 (M-704): the **free-variable walk** for closure capture analysis. Collects the"] # [doc = " single-segment `Expr::Path` names occurring in `e` that are **not** bound by an enclosing binder"] # [doc = " *within* `e` (`Let`, `Match` arm patterns, `For`, inner `Lambda` params) — appended to `out` in"] # [doc = " **first-occurrence order** (a total deterministic order — §4A.3 / G2), each once (`seen` dedups)."] # [doc = " `bound` carries the names currently in scope inside `e` (seeded with the lambda's own parameter"] # [doc = " by the caller); a name in `bound` is local, not free. gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The depth-tracked worker behind [`free_vars`] (M-866): `depth` counts the live nesting of this"] # [doc = " traversal's own recursive descent (not any semantic property of `e`), charged on entry and"] # [doc = " checked against [`MAX_WALK_DEPTH`] before any further recursion — mirrors"] # [doc = " [`crate::totality::walk_expr_at`]'s M-674 discipline (same budget, same reified-counter"] # [doc = " mechanism, DRY): rather than rely on the host call stack (a resource, not a semantic limit) to"] # [doc = " bound the walk, the pass carries this explicit budget and refuses past it with a clean"] # [doc = " [`WalkDepthExceeded`], never a host-stack overflow."] fn free_vars_at (e : & Expr , bound : & mut BTreeSet < String > , seen : & mut BTreeSet < String > , out : & mut Vec < String > , depth : u32 ,) -> Result < () , WalkDepthExceeded > { let depth = depth + 1 ; if depth > MAX_WALK_DEPTH { return Err (WalkDepthExceeded { limit : MAX_WALK_DEPTH , }) ; } match e { Expr :: Path (p) => { if p . 0 . len () == 1 { let n = & p . 0 [0] ; if ! bound . contains (n) && seen . insert (n . clone ()) { out . push (n . clone ()) ; } } } Expr :: Lit (Literal :: List (elems)) => { for el in elems { free_vars_at (el , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; } } Expr :: Lit (_) => { } Expr :: Let { name , bound : b , body , .. } => { free_vars_at (b , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; let was = bound . insert (name . clone ()) ; free_vars_at (body , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; } Expr :: Match { scrutinee , arms } => { free_vars_at (scrutinee , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; for arm in arms { let mut added : Vec < String > = Vec :: new () ; pattern_binders (& arm . pattern , bound , & mut added) ? ; free_vars_at (& arm . body , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; for n in added { bound . remove (& n) ; } } } Expr :: For { x , xs , acc , init , body , } => { free_vars_at (xs , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; free_vars_at (init , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; let ax = bound . insert (x . clone ()) ; let aacc = bound . insert (acc . clone ()) ; free_vars_at (body , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; if aacc { bound . remove (acc) ; } if ax { bound . remove (x) ; } } Expr :: Swap { value , .. } => free_vars_at (value , bound , seen , out , depth) ? , Expr :: WithParadigm { body , .. } => free_vars_at (body , bound , seen , out , depth) ? , Expr :: Wild (b) | Expr :: Spore (b) | Expr :: Consume (b) | Expr :: Wrapping (b) | Expr :: Try (b) => { free_vars_at (b , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; } Expr :: Colony (hyphae) => { for h in hyphae { free_vars_at (& h . body , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; } } Expr :: Lambda { params , body } => { let mut added : Vec < String > = Vec :: new () ; for p in params { if bound . insert (p . name . clone ()) { added . push (p . name . clone ()) ; } } free_vars_at (body , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; for n in added { bound . remove (& n) ; } } Expr :: App { head , args } => { free_vars_at (head , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; for a in args { free_vars_at (a , bound , seen , out , depth) ? ; } } Expr :: Fuse { left , right } => { free_vars_at (left , bound , seen , out , depth) ? gaps.snippet: # [doc = " If `ty` is a **finite, enumerable** domain in v0's sense (a registered `Data` type every one of"] # [doc = " whose constructors takes zero fields — the `Bool`-shape), return every value of that domain;"] # [doc = " `None` for anything else (an unregistered name, a type with fielded constructors, a repr type,"] # [doc = " an uninhabited type) — never a guess at a partial enumeration (G2)."] fn enumerate_finite_domain (ty : & Ty , types : & BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > ,) -> Option < Vec < crate :: eval :: L1Value > > { let Ty :: Data (name , _args) = ty else { return None ; } ; let local = crate :: checkty :: ty_local_name (name) ; let info = crate :: checkty :: lookup_data_home_checked (types , name , "check_fuse_laws") . ok () ? ; if info . ctors . is_empty () || info . ctors . iter () . any (| c | ! c . fields . is_empty ()) { return None ; } Some (info . ctors . iter () . map (| c | crate :: eval :: L1Value :: Data { ty : local . to_owned () , ctor : c . name . clone () , fields : std :: sync :: Arc :: new (vec ! []) , }) . collect () ,) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " F-A2 — the semilattice-law checker. Walks every registered instance of the [`TRAIT_NAME`]"] # [doc = " trait; for each whose `for_ty` is a [`enumerate_finite_domain`] domain, exhaustively checks"] # [doc = " idempotence, commutativity, and associativity by evaluating `join` (via a scratch"] # [doc = " [`Evaluator`]) over every value/pair/triple of that domain. The first law violation found is"] # [doc = " returned as a never-silent [`CheckError`] naming the law, a witness, and the `impl`'s site"] # [doc = " (G2); a non-enumerable `for_ty` is silently **skipped** — not silently *accepted as lawful*,"] # [doc = " just left unchecked (documented above, VR-5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " A [`CheckError`] naming the violated law + witness for the first unlawful `Fuse` instance"] # [doc = " found (site order = `BTreeMap` iteration order over `(trait, head)`, i.e. deterministic)."] pub (crate) fn check_fuse_laws (types : & BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > , fns : & BTreeMap < String , FnDecl > , traits : & BTreeMap < String , TraitInfo > , instances : & BTreeMap < (String , String) , InstanceInfo > , impls : & BTreeMap < (String , String) , Vec < FnDecl > > ,) -> Result < () , CheckError > { for ((trait_name , head) , methods) in impls { if trait_name != TRAIT_NAME { continue ; } let Some (inst) = instances . get (& (trait_name . clone () , head . clone ())) else { continue ; } ; let Some (join) = methods . iter () . find (| m | m . sig . name == "join") else { continue ; } ; let Some (domain) = enumerate_finite_domain (& inst . for_ty , types) else { continue ; } ; check_laws_over_domain (& inst . for_ty , join , & domain , types , fns , traits , instances , impls ,) ? ; } Ok (()) }

# [doc = " Exhaustively check the three semilattice laws for `join` over every value of `domain`"] # [doc = " (`for_ty`'s enumerated values), evaluating through a scratch [`Env`] that carries every"] # [doc = " already-checked registry (so `join`'s body may call ordinary top-level fns / other trait"] # [doc = " methods exactly as it would in the real program) plus one synthetic entry — `join` itself,"] # [doc = " under [`PROBE_FN`] — so [`Evaluator::call`] can invoke it directly without going through the"] # [doc = " generic trait-method call-resolution surface (unnecessary here: `for_ty` is already concrete,"] # [doc = " so there is exactly one candidate method, no ambiguity to resolve)."] # [allow (clippy :: too_many_arguments)] fn check_laws_over_domain (for_ty : & Ty , join : & FnDecl , domain : & [crate :: eval :: L1Value] , types : & BTreeMap < String , DataInfo > , fns : & BTreeMap < String , FnDecl > , traits : & BTreeMap < String , TraitInfo > , instances : & BTreeMap < (String , String) , InstanceInfo > , impls : & BTreeMap < (String , String) , Vec < FnDecl > > ,) -> Result < () , CheckError > { let mut probe_fns = fns . clone () ; probe_fns . insert (PROBE_FN . to_owned () , FnDecl { vis : Vis :: Private , thaw : false , tier : None , sig : join . sig . clone () , body : join . body . clone () , } ,) ; let scratch = Env { types : types . clone () , fns : probe_fns , totality : BTreeMap :: new () , traits : traits . clone () , instances : instances . clone () , impls : impls . clone () , lower_rules : BTreeMap :: new () , derived_provenance : BTreeMap :: new () , via_provenance : BTreeMap :: new () , } ; let evaluator = Evaluator :: new (& scratch) ; let site = format ! ("impl Fuse[{for_ty}] for {for_ty}") ; let call = | a : & crate :: eval :: L1Value , b : & crate :: eval :: L1Value | -> Result < crate :: eval :: L1Value , CheckError > { evaluator . call (PROBE_FN , vec ! [a . clone () , b . clone ()]) . map_err (| e | { CheckError :: new (& site , format ! ("could not evaluate `Fuse::join` for `{for_ty}` while checking the \
                         semilattice laws (DN-58 §A.4; RFC-0008 RT6): {e} — a `join` that cannot \
                         be evaluated over its own declared domain cannot be certified lawful \
                         (never-silent — G2)") ,) }) } ; for a in domain { let got = call (a , a) ? ; if & got != a { return Err (CheckError :: new (& site , format ! ("`Fuse::join` for `{for_ty}` violates idempotence (RFC-0008 RT6 / DN-58 §A.1): \
                     join({a:?}, {a:?}) = {got:?}, expected {a:?} — a semilattice-law violation is \
                     refused at definition, never a silent accept (G2/M-965)") ,)) ; } } for a in domain { for b in domain { let ab = call (a , b) ? ; let ba = call (b , a) ? ; if ab != ba { return Err (CheckError :: new (& site , format ! ("`Fuse::join` for `{for_ty}` violates commutativity (RFC-0008 RT6 / DN-58 \
                         §A.1): join({a:?}, {b:?}) = {ab:?} but join({b:?}, {a:?}) = {ba:?} — a \
                         semilattice-law violation is refused at definition, never a silent accept \
                         (G2/M-965)") ,)) ; } } } for a in domain { for b in domain { for c in domain { let ab = call (a , b) ? ; let ab_c = call (& ab , c) ? ; let bc = call (b , c) ? ; let a_bc = call (a , & bc) ? ; if ab_c != a_bc { return Err (CheckError :: new (& site , format ! ("`Fuse::join` for `{for_ty}` violates associativity (RFC-0008 RT6 / \
                             DN-58 §A.1): join(join({a:?}, {b:?}), {c:?}) = {ab_c:?} but \
                             join({a:?}, join({b:?}, {c:?})) = {a_bc:?} — a semilattice-law \
                             violation is refused at definition, never a silent accept (G2/M-965)") ,)) ; } } } } Ok (()) }
